GoRocky/core.title: Feature/wm go forms ds
GoRocky/core.description: # feat(forms/wm): port WM assessment to GoForms DS **Branch:** `feature/WM-GoForms-DS` → `main` **Commits:** 12 ahead of `main` **Scope:** `apps/web` (renderer + WM custom screens), `apps/backend` (5 migrations), `packages/goforms-ds` ## Summary Ports the `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2` (Weight Management) form onto the GoForms Design System renderer and adds the WM-specific custom screens — landing, consent, contact details, transition, disqualification. Along the way, the renderer gained a handful of generally-useful fixes (back-nav across content-only screens, optional `DateInput` label, custom-section validation skip, metadata-driven reassurance), and the form template was iteratively refined through five migrations (346 → 350). The flow now renders end-to-end through `DSFormView` with the DS visual language: stagger reveals, neutral/orange tokens, single-question screens, reassurance strips on sensitive sections, and the gradient primary button. The "details" capture step has been moved to the **end** of the form ("You're all set! Just one last thing.") so the user only commits PII once they've cleared eligibility. A small side-fix swaps the inline ED-colored CTAs in the GoSlim personalized-plan flow for the goforms-ds gradient `Button` so the two checkout funnels look consistent. ## Changes by area ### WM custom screens (new) - `WmLandingPage.tsx` — vertical-aware hero + entry CTA - `WmConsentPage.tsx` — DS-styled consent screen - `WmContactDetails.tsx` — first/last/phone/email capture, centered on desktop, end-of-form copy - `WmTransition.tsx` — momentum/stat screens with word-by-word stagger (wraps on any width) - `WmDisqualificationPage.tsx` — empathetic disqualification UX Registered in `apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/wl/index.ts`; routed through `FormViewRouter` → `DSFormView`. ### Renderer (`apps/web/src/components/forms/`) - **Back navigation** skips content-only transition screens (`isContentOnly` flag) so users can always reach the previous question. Interactive custom screens (landing/consent/contact) remain normally navigable. - **Validation** skips custom-component sections to avoid stale-answer races. - **ReassuranceStrip** is metadata-driven (`showReassurance` flag on sections) rather than hard-coded to a section key. - **DSFormView** uses a ref to `handleNext` so the auto-advance `setTimeout` always calls the latest handler (stale-closure fix). - **FieldRendererV2** suppresses the DOB field's own label when it's the first field on the screen (the section heading already serves as the label). - **Inter-field gap** bumped from 16px → 32px (Law of Proximity). - WM vertical no longer auto-advances; wiring kept for future verticals. ### Design system (`packages/goforms-ds`) - `DateInput.label` is now optional — lets callers omit a duplicate label when the question heading is already rendered as an h2. - `verticals.ts` — minor WM tuning. ### Backend migrations | # | What | | ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `346` | Wire DS custom components to WM sections (`customComponent` metadata). | | `347` | Split `weight-loss-goals` → two single-question screens (`weight-loss-goals` + new `motivation`). | | `348` | Add `showReassurance` flag to `medical-history` and `medical-background` sections. | | `349` | Fix body-measurements heading + embed units into question prompts ("Height in cm"), null out redundant help. | | `350` | Drop optional `hip_size_inches` / `waist_size_inches` questions; move `personal-info` to the end of the form. | a Every migration ships a matching rollback in `apps/backend/src/database/migrations/rollback/`. ### Side fixes (GoSlim & ED) - `StickyFooterCTA` + `ProductRecommendation` "Continue with …" CTA now use the goforms-ds `Button` primary variant (radial gradient via `--ds-btn-primary-bg`, consistent hover/active/loading). - ED landing page logo no longer routes through the brand provider — always the GoRocky SVG. ### Env templates `apps/admin/.env.example`, `apps/backend/.env.example`, `apps/webflow/.env.example` get the Aperture DAM, blog ISR revalidation, Cal.com care-manager event-id, and Turnstile placeholders documented (no behavior change; these are example-file additions only). ## Test plan - [ ] **WM form happy path** — land → consent → answer every section (including motivation as its own screen) → details capture at the very end → submission. Confirm no auto-advance. - [ ] **WM back navigation** — from any question, Back lands on the previous **interactive** screen (skips transitions). - [ ] **Body measurements section** — only height (cm) and weight (kg) render; heading reads "Your body measurements"; no hip/waist fields. - [ ] **Disqualification path** — trigger via a hard-stop answer; verify empathetic page, `/consultation` link, no broken nav. - [ ] **Mobile + desktop** — details form is horizontally centered on ≥768px (image column commented out); single-column on mobile. - [ ] **Reassurance** — strip renders only on `medical-history` and `medical-background` (flag-driven), not elsewhere. - [ ] **DateInput** — DOB on the first screen renders with no duplicate label. - [ ] **GoSlim CTAs** — `/forms/goslim-assessment-v2` → personalized plan: gradient Buttons in sticky footer and the inline product CTA, with the DS hover/active/loading states. - [ ] **ED landing** — GoRocky logo renders regardless of brand cookie/subdomain. - [ ] **Migrations** — `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate:status` shows 346–350 applied; rollback chain (350 → 346) re-applies cleanly. ## Out of scope / follow-ups - Re-enabling the desktop hero image in `WmContactDetails` (currently commented out — kept the `Image` import so it's a one-line revert when product decides). - The `--color-ed-*` tokens still survive in places outside personalized-plan; broader cleanup is its own pass. - DS `Button`'s loading label is hard-coded to "Submitting…" — if we want a per-call override (e.g. "Processing…") that's a small DS extension. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --- Need help on this PR? GoRocky/core.title: feat(analytics): add QR V2 funnel attribution
GoRocky/core.description: Adds a QR V2 sub-tab to the admin Funnel page that scopes the QR conversion funnel to /welcome-v2, alongside the existing QR tab now scoped to /welcome only — so the two landing pages can be compared side-by-side. - web: welcome-v2 CTA event now stamps page: '/welcome-v2' (was '/welcome', which would have mis-attributed clicks) - backend: GET /analytics/funnel/form/qr accepts an optional welcomePath query param; the SQL filter is parameterized via \$3 with ::text casts, and the DTO validates the value matches a URL-path shape - admin: new QR V2 sub-tab and detail panel; QR tab now passes welcomePath=/welcome explicitly so the two tabs are disjoint --- Need help on this PR? GoRocky/core.title: Feature/blog cms
GoRocky/core.description:
# Blog CMS — `feature/blog-cms`

Replaces the static `apps/webflow/src/data/blog-posts.ts` array (47 posts, ~2,200 lines) with a full admin-managed Blog CMS. Editors can create, draft-preview, and publish posts from the admin UI; the public webflow site reads from a backend API via ISR and renders with the existing V7 design.

**Scope**: 85 commits, ~9.7K insertions, 90 files. Backend module + repository + audit + Aperture proxy, admin editor (15+ components), webflow rendering pipeline, two `pnpm install` workspace cycles, four DB migrations.

## Why

- Blog content lived in a 2,200-line TS file. Any copy change required a deploy. Non-engineers couldn't author. The file diverged across branches and merge-conflicted regularly.
- Marketing wanted an in-house authoring tool that didn't require a developer in the loop.
- The V7 redesign on `main` was built for richer post structure (subsections, per-section FAQs/references/CTAs) than the legacy data could express.

## What ships

### Backend — `apps/backend`

- **`blog_posts` table** (migration `298`) — full SEO + content + author/reviewer columns, `status` enum (`draft|published|archived`), `published_at`, per-row `preview_token`, `version`, `featured`, JSONB `sections[]` / `faq[]` / `related_articles[]`.
- **Audit log integration** — new `blog_post` entity type (migration `299`); every create / update / publish / unpublish / delete is recorded by `BlogAuditService` with structured diffs. Per-field change tracking (e.g. SEO audit event only fires when an SEO field actually changed).
- **`BlogModule`** — NestJS controller + service + repository with full CRUD, `publish` / `unpublish` workflow, optimistic locking via `version`, transactional publish/unpublish/delete (`BEGIN` → repo op → audit write → `COMMIT`), and per-row preview tokens.
- **Public response sanitization** — `preview_token` is stripped from `GET /blog`, `GET /blog/:slug`, `GET /blog/preview/:token` responses via a `PublicBlogPostRow` type so unauthenticated callers can't harvest tokens for posts they later get unpublished.
- **`@Public()` admin endpoints** — `/blog/admin` (paginated list, any status, requires `blog:view`) and `/blog/admin/:id` for the editor; `/blog` (published only) and `/blog/preview/:token` for the public site.
- **ISR revalidation hook** — backend pings the webflow `/api/revalidate` endpoint after publish/unpublish so cached pages refresh within ~60 s without a redeploy. Fails closed if `REVALIDATE_SECRET` is unset.
- **Aperture DAM proxy module** — server-side proxy to the internal Aperture asset manager. MIME allowlist explicitly excludes SVG (executable JS payload risk). Lets the admin upload via the existing internal asset CDN without exposing the Bearer token to the browser.
- **Permission `blog:publish`** — added to `access-control.ts` and `PERMISSION_RESOURCES` in `role-permissions.service.ts` so the admin role editor renders the checkbox. Granted by default to all admin roles.
- **Seed script** — `apps/backend/scripts/seed-blog-posts.ts` imports all 47 legacy posts from `blog-posts.ts` into the new table.
- **Migration 304** — renames the subsection type enum value `medication` → `highlight` across all stored JSONB. Reversible.

### Admin — `apps/admin`

#### Editor structure
- **`/blog`** list page with status filter, search, and pagination.
- **`/blog/new`** + **`/blog/[id]`** routes wired to `BlogPostEditor`.
- **`BlogPostEditor`** (the centerpiece) is split across four tabs: Content, Author / Reviewer, SEO, Metadata. Built on `react-hook-form` + Zod (`createBlogPostSchema` / `updateBlogPostSchema`).

#### Content tab
- **Title / Slug / Short Description** at the top, with auto-slug from title and a "Regenerate" button (locked once published — slug changes break SEO).
- **Summary (Key Takeaways)** — accepts plain dash-bullet input (`- foo`) which the renderer auto-converts to `<ul>`. Live char counter under the field.
- **Introduction** — TipTap rich-text editor.
- **Main image / Blog listing image** — URL inputs with inline 128×80 click-to-open thumbnails and a "Failed to load" badge for broken URLs. Width/height/alt text fields next to the main image.
- **Sections list** with collapsible cards, drag-style reorder buttons (`↑` / `↓`), heading + subtitle + section image + body. Each section can have:
  - **Subsections** — Numbered (renders as styled `[01]` / `[02]` `<ol>`) or Highlight cards (image + title + body).
  - **FAQs** — collapsed by default when empty; expanded when populated.
  - **References** — TipTap rich-text editor (no headings) so editors can attach links to citation titles.
  - **CTA** — heading, button label, link, and an optional `learnMoreLink` override for the secondary "Learn More" button.
- **Sticky TOC sidebar** on the left rail (matches the live V7 layout), collapses an entry's chevron jumps the editor to that section card.
- **Legacy fields disclosure** — body1 / body2 / postReferences / faqJson / cta1 / cta2 surfaced for Phase 6 posts only, with a clear amber warning panel explaining the alternation behavior.

#### Author / Reviewer tab
- **Preset dropdowns** for both Author and Reviewer (constants in `lib/constants/blog-people.ts`) — picking a preset auto-fills name + image + title + bio. Free-text fallback for one-off contributors.
- Reviewer image / title / bio fields drive the doctor profile card on the live V7 layout.

#### SEO tab (2-column layout)
- Left: Meta Title / Meta Description (with live char counters and color-coded warnings for SERP truncation), Focus Keyword, OG / Twitter image fields, Twitter Card type, Canonical URL.
- Right: **Live OpenGraph preview** that renders a Facebook/LinkedIn/X-style share card from form values. Updates as you type. Solves the "we have to deploy and use the FB sharing debugger" problem.
- Brand suffix (`| GoRocky`) auto-appended to Meta Title at render time, so editors don't have to type it.

#### Metadata tab
- **Cascading category dropdowns** (Category Page → Category → Subcategory) driven by the same `BLOG_CATEGORY_PAGES` config the public site uses.
- **Read time**, **featured** flag.
- **Related Articles** — replaced the legacy slug textbox with a Select fetched live via `useBlogs({ status: 'published' })`. Posts in the current category are grouped at the top; the rest below. Self-link is filtered out, duplicates are blocked.

#### Editor chrome
- **Sticky header** with title, status badge, "Unsaved changes" badge (amber, dot indicator), Guide / Preview / Publish / Unpublish / Delete / Save buttons.
- **Cmd/Ctrl+S** saves through the form's submit handler (so validation runs).
- **`beforeunload`** guard if there are unsaved changes.
- **Error count badge** on each tab when validation fails. Submit-failure auto-switches to the first invalid tab and scrolls + focuses the offending field.
- **Preview button** opens `/blog/preview/<token>` on the webflow app. Shows "(last saved)" hint and tooltip when the form is dirty so editors know they're previewing the saved version.
- **Publish-with-unsaved-changes** confirmation dialog with explicit copy ("Publish the last saved version?" / "Cancel (keep editing)").
- **Slug-change warning** — amber inline note flagging the old → new slug for drafts that were saved at least once.
- **`buildBlogPreviewUrl` helper** — uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBFLOW_URL` when set; falls back to `localhost:3004` on dev only; toasts a clear error on staging/prod when the env var is missing instead of silently linking to localhost.

#### Editor primitives + helpers
- **`RichTextField`** — TipTap-based editor used for Introduction, body content, FAQ answers, References, and Section subsection bodies. Toolbar: Bold, Italic, H3/H4 (configurable), bullet/numbered lists, blockquote, link, superscript, clear formatting, undo/redo.
- **Notion-style floating link popover** — opens when the cursor lands on a link; toolbar link button or `Cmd/Ctrl+K` opens it in editing mode against the current selection. URL input + Apply, plus open-in-tab / copy / unlink actions. Validator accepts http/https, `mailto:`, `tel:`, `/relative-paths`, and `#anchors`.
- **`LabelWithTip`** — every form field has an info icon next to its label with a hover tooltip explaining what the field does, where it appears on the live site, and any fallbacks.
- **`BlogGuidelinesDrawer`** — Radix Sheet triggered by a "Guide" button in the editor header. Renders sections (Image sizes, SEO essentials, Writing & structure, CTAs) from a typed source-of-truth in `lib/blog-guidelines.ts`. Sections support `paragraph` / `list` / `table` / `callout` block types so updates are a single-file PR.

### Webflow public site — `apps/webflow`

- **`/blog`, `/blog/[slug]`, `/blog-category/[category]`** now fetch from `getPublishedPosts` / `getPostBySlug` (server-side, ISR `revalidate: 60`). Static `blog-posts.ts` import is gone; the file remains for the seed script + as the type source for `BlogPost`.
- **`/blog/preview/[token]`** — token-gated draft preview route, `noindex`, with a sticky "DRAFT PREVIEW" banner. Uses the same V7 layout as live posts.
- **`BlogPostV7`** — the redesign component from `main` is consumed unchanged for posts that use `sections[]`. CMS-aware additions:
  - **Subsections** rendered after each section's body (Numbered cards via `.blog-v7-numbered-steps` CSS counters; Highlight cards as image+title+body cards).
  - **Aggregated FAQs / References** — `parseBlogPost` flattens per-section FAQs into the top-level FAQ accordion and concatenates per-section references into the "Article References" accordion. References render as `[1]` / `[2]` … via CSS counters on a `.blog-v7-references` `<ol>`.
  - **Per-section CTA** is the only CTA rendering surface; legacy `post.cta1` / `post.cta2` data still hydrates onto the first / second / etc. sections at render time using the same alternating distribution the original `ctas[i % ctas.length]` produced. New posts use only the per-section model.
  - **Per-section Learn More override** — secondary CTA button URL prefers `section.cta.learnMoreLink`, then a category-derived map (`/treatments/gorocky` for ED, `gofuller` for Hair Loss, `goslim` for Weight Loss), then the homepage.
- **Section images** render at natural aspect ratio (capped at 640px tall, centered) instead of the previous fixed 180/280px landscape crop that distorted portraits.
- **Plain-text-friendly rendering** — `plainTextToHtml` converts editor input without HTML tags into proper paragraphs / `<ul>` (dash bullets) / `<ol>` (`1.`/`1)`/`[1]` numbered prefixes), with `<` / `>` / `&` escaped to prevent XSS. Posts with HTML already in their fields pass through unchanged for backwards compat with seeded posts.
- **Title `| GoRocky` suffix** auto-appended in `generateMetadata` (idempotent — skipped if the editor already typed it).
- **Dynamic sitemap** + **`/robots.ts`** pull live published slugs. `robots.ts` disallows `/components`, `/order/`, `/blog/preview/`, `/prototypes/`.
- **Full SEO JSON-LD** — `Article`, `MedicalWebPage` (when reviewed), `FAQPage` (when sections contain FAQs), `BreadcrumbList`, `CollectionPage` for listings.
- **`blog-api.ts`** mapper converts the backend payload to the shared `BlogPost` shape; `sections: null` from the DB becomes `undefined` so the type system stays clean.

### Permissions
- **`blog:publish`** action surfaced in the admin role-permissions UI (was previously enforced server-side but invisible in the role editor — admins couldn't grant or revoke it without a SQL update).
- **Publish/Unpublish buttons in the editor** render unconditionally; backend `@RequirePermissions('blog', ['publish'])` is the actual enforcement, with a tooltip on the button explaining the action will fail at the server when the role lacks the perm.

### DX / quality
- **Test coverage** added to backend for: `version`-guarded update, atomic delete-with-audit, GET `/blog/:slug` slug-existence timing sidechannel.
- **Env vars documented** in `apps/backend/.env.example` (`APERTURE_API_URL`, `APERTURE_API_KEY`, `REVALIDATE_SECRET`) and `apps/admin/.env.example` (`NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBFLOW_URL`).
- **Two clean merges from `main`** during development; rebase-clean against current main as of this PR.

## Migrations to run

In order:
1. **`298_blog_posts.sql`** — creates the `blog_posts` table.
2. **`299_audit_log_blog_post_entity.sql`** — extends the `audit_log_entity_type_chk` constraint to include `blog_post`.
3. **`304_blog_subsection_rename_medication_to_highlight.sql`** — renames the subsection enum value in existing JSONB sections (data-only, idempotent, reversible).

Then seed the legacy posts:

```bash
pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend tsx scripts/seed-blog-posts.ts
```

Each migration ships with a paired rollback under `migrations/rollback/`.

## Required env vars

**Backend** (`apps/backend/.env`):
- `APERTURE_API_URL`, `APERTURE_API_KEY`
- `REVALIDATE_SECRET`

**Webflow** (`apps/webflow/.env`):
- `BACKEND_URL` (server-side fetches)
- `REVALIDATE_SECRET` (must match backend)

**Admin** (`apps/admin/.env`):
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBFLOW_URL` (used by the editor's Preview button — required on staging/prod or the button will toast a clear error)

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend test src/modules/blog` — 15 tests pass.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend exec tsc --noEmit`, `@gorocky/admin`, `@gorocky/webflow` — all green.
- [ ] Apply migrations 298, 299, 304; run the seed script; visit `/blog` on the webflow site and confirm 47 posts render with the V7 design.
- [ ] In admin, open a draft post, edit a field, save → updated copy appears on `/blog/<slug>` within 60 s (ISR).
- [ ] Create a new draft → click Preview → V7 layout renders with the draft banner; not indexable.
- [ ] Publish the draft → public `/blog/<slug>` returns it after revalidation.
- [ ] Unpublish a post → public route 404s after revalidation; admin still sees it.
- [ ] Audit log shows entries for create / update / publish / unpublish / delete with correct actor + diff.
- [ ] Editor image fields show thumbnails for valid URLs and a "Failed to load" badge for broken ones.
- [ ] Sections, subsections (numbered + highlight), per-section FAQs and References, per-section CTA all render correctly on `/blog/<slug>` and `/blog/preview/<token>`.
- [ ] Legacy posts (with `cta1*` / `cta2*` data) still render their alternating CTAs across sections.
- [ ] Admin role editor shows 5/5 for the Blog resource (View / Create / Update / Delete / Publish).
- [ ] `Cmd/Ctrl+K` in any rich-text editor opens the link popover.
- [ ] Guide drawer opens from the editor header and renders the four sections.

## Known follow-ups

Tracked in the branch's todo list, deferred to keep this PR focused:

1. **Map `authorImage` / `reviewerImage` / `reviewerTitle` / `reviewerBio` in `apps/webflow/src/lib/blog-api.ts → mapPost()`.** All four fields exist in the DB and are editable in the admin, but the current mapper drops them on the way to the V7 renderer. Four-line fix; V7 falls back to the default reviewer until then.
2. **Add `featuredOrder` + `globalFeatured` end-to-end.** `BlogListingV2` reads these to curate the listing hero + side cards; today everything falls back to `featured`/first-post. Needs a small migration (`featured_order INT`, `global_featured BOOLEAN`), repo column lists, admin schema/UI, mapper.
3. **Replace `window.prompt` for new-link entry.** Already done for editing existing links (Notion-style popover); the toolbar's "add new link" path still uses the popover too — both are unified. Marked complete; flag for any regressions.
4. **Standalone `/blog/help` page** — the `BlogGuidelinesDrawer` content module is reusable; a public-facing `/blog/help` route would render the same data. Skipped because the drawer covers ~all the use cases editors actually hit.
5. **Webflow blog page light-mode pass.** All page colors are hard-coded to a light-mode palette (`#FAF9F7` cream surface + dark earthtone text). The page renders identically in both modes today (no `dark:` variants). If the webflow site's chrome ever switches between light/dark, the blog page will look out-of-place against the light-mode chrome. Punted as a separate styling task.

## Notable decisions / non-obvious choices

- **Cart-style legacy preservation**: legacy `cta1*` / `cta2*` columns are *not* dropped from the `blog_posts` schema. The renderer hydrates them onto sections at render time so the 47 seeded posts keep their hero CTAs without manual editor work. The admin form's `defaultValues` keeps the columns alive on save so opening + saving a legacy post doesn't strip them.
- **Token rotation**: this PR does *not* rotate `preview_token` for existing rows. If anyone scraped the public list before the `preview_token` strip landed, those tokens still work. Acceptable risk for marketing content; flag if you'd prefer a one-shot rotation script.
- **No DB CHECK constraint on subsection.type.** The Zod schema (admin) and TypeScript union (renderer) enforce `'numbered' | 'highlight'`. Adding a JSONB CHECK is possible but adds migration noise for low value.
- **Tailwind v4 with no Typography plugin.** All `prose-*` modifiers are no-ops in this project, which caused two bugs during development (editor link styling, callout text contrast). Editor + drawer styling now uses direct CSS in `globals.css` (`.tiptap a`) and `text-foreground` tokens instead of `prose-*` chains.

## File breakdown (rough)

| Area | Files | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Backend module | ~15 | controller, service, repository, audit, DTOs, module |
| Backend migrations | 6 | 298 (table), 299 (audit type), 304 (rename), plus 3 rollbacks |
| Aperture proxy | ~3 | controller + service + module |
| Admin editor | ~25 | `BlogPostEditor` + 4 tab files + helpers (LabelWithTip, ImageUrlField, OpenGraphPreview, MoveButtons, RelatedArticleCombobox, SectionsTOC, RichTextField, BlogGuidelinesDrawer, etc.) |
| Admin routes | 3 | `/blog`, `/blog/new`, `/blog/[id]` |
| Admin constants / hooks / API | ~6 | `blog-people.ts`, `blog-categories.ts`, `blog-guidelines.ts`, `blog-preview-url.ts`, `use-blog.ts`, `lib/api/blog.ts` |
| Webflow rendering | ~6 | `BlogPostV7` extension, `blog-parser.ts`, `blog-api.ts`, `blog-html.ts`, slug page, preview page |
| Tests | ~3 | repo + service + audit specs |
| Docs | 4 | env examples + this summary + openspec changes archived |
GoRocky/core.summary: Replaces the static `apps/webflow/src/data/blog-posts.ts` array (47 posts, ~2,200 lines) with a full admin-managed Blog CMS. Editors can create, draft-preview, and publish posts from the admin UI; the public webflow site reads from a backend API via ISR and renders with the existing V7 design. **Sco

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/blog cms v1 (staging test)
GoRocky/core.description:
# Blog CMS — `feature/blog-cms` Replaces the static `blog-posts.ts` array with a full admin-managed Blog CMS. Editors can create, draft-preview, and publish posts; the public `/blog` site reads from the API via ISR; the existing V2/V7 design from `main` is preserved end-to-end. ## Why Blog content was hard-coded in `apps/webflow/src/data/blog-posts.ts` (47+ posts, ~2,200 lines). Any copy change required a code deploy, non-engineers couldn't author, and the file diverged across branches. The CMS lets the content team own the publishing pipeline. ## What ships ### Backend (`apps/backend`) - **`blog_posts` table** (migration `298`) with full SEO + content + author/reviewer columns, `status` enum (`draft|published|archived`), `published_at`, `preview_token`, `version`, `featured`, JSONB `sections[]` / `faq[]` / `related_articles[]`. - **`BlogModule`** — controller + service + repository with full CRUD, `publish` / `unpublish` workflow, optimistic locking via `version`, and per-row preview tokens. - **Audit log integration** — new `blog_post` entity type (migration `299`); every create / update / publish / unpublish / delete is recorded by `BlogAuditService`. - **ISR revalidation hook** — backend pings `/api/revalidate` on the webflow app after publish/unpublish so cached pages refresh without a redeploy. Secret shared via `REVALIDATE_SECRET`. - **`@Public()` admin endpoints** — `/blog/admin` (paginated list, any status) and `/blog/admin/:id` for the editor; `/blog` (published only) and `/blog/preview/:token` for the public site. - **Permission `blog:publish`** added to access control; `admin` role gets it by default. - **Aperture proxy module** — server-side proxy to the internal Aperture DAM so the admin can fetch image assets without exposing the Bearer token to the browser. - **Seed script** (`scripts/seed-blog-posts.ts`) — one-shot import of all 47 legacy posts from `blog-posts.ts` into the new table. ### Admin (`apps/admin`) #### Routes & shell - **`/blog`** list page with status filter, search, and pagination. - **`/blog/new`** + **`/blog/[id]`** routes wired to `BlogPostEditor`. - **TanStack Query hooks** (`use-blog.ts`) with cache invalidation around mutations. #### `BlogPostEditor` — editor architecture Built on `react-hook-form` + Zod (`createBlogPostSchema` / `updateBlogPostSchema`). The editor is split into 15 focused files under `apps/admin/src/components/blog/`: ``` blog/ ├── BlogPostEditor.tsx shell, header, form wiring, dialogs ├── RichTextField.tsx TipTap editor wrapper ├── helpers/ │ ├── ImageUrlField.tsx image URL input + thumbnail preview │ ├── LabelWithTip.tsx label with hover tooltip │ ├── MoveButtons.tsx up/down reorder buttons │ ├── OpenGraphPreview.tsx live OG share-card preview │ ├── RelatedArticleCombobox.tsx cmdk-based typeahead picker │ ├── SectionsTOC.tsx sticky right-rail table of contents │ └── slugify.ts ├── sections/ │ ├── SectionCard.tsx one section + nested faqs/cta/refs │ └── SubsectionCard.tsx numbered/medication block └── tabs/ ├── ContentTab.tsx core fields + sections list + legacy ├── AuthorTab.tsx author + reviewer ├── SeoTab.tsx SEO + OG preview ├── CtasTab.tsx global CTA1/CTA2 └── MetadataTab.tsx categorization + related articles ``` The form has structured repeatable fields for `sections[]`, per-section `subsections[]` (numbered list / medication card variants), per-section `faqs[]`, per-section `references` and `cta`, plus `relatedArticles[]`. #### Rich-text editing (TipTap) - All long-form fields (Summary, Introduction, section Body Content, subsection Body, FAQ Answer) use a custom `RichTextField` built on **TipTap v3** (`@tiptap/react` + `starter-kit` + `extension-link` + `extension-superscript` + `extension-placeholder`). - Toolbar: bold, italic, H3/H4, bullet/numbered lists, blockquote, link (with prompt dialog), superscript (for reference numbers), clear formatting, undo/redo. `compact` mode hides the toolbar for FAQ answers + subsection bodies. - Heading levels restricted to **H3/H4** by default since H2 is the section heading itself; legacy `body1`/`body2` fields keep raw textareas. - Legacy HTML compatibility verified: every tag in the existing 47 posts (`

`, ``, ``, ``, ``, `

    `, `
      `, `
    • `, `
      `, `

      `) maps to a TipTap extension. Webflow's `id=""` attribute artifacts get stripped on first re-save. - SSR-safe (`immediatelyRender: false`); syncs on external value changes without resetting the cursor mid-typing; output is normalized so empty paragraphs / zero-width chars don't trip RHF's `isDirty`. - Links open in new tabs with `rel="noopener noreferrer"`. #### Section reorder + sticky table of contents - **Real up/down reorder buttons** on sections, subsections, FAQs, and related articles using `useFieldArray.swap`. (Replaces the previous `` icons that didn't actually drag.) - **Sticky `SectionsTOC` sidebar** rendered in `ContentTab` at `lg+` widths. Lists each section by heading (or "Section N" if blank). - Clicking a TOC item expands the section if collapsed, then smoothly scrolls it into view (with `scroll-mt-20` so the header isn't flush with the page top). - Active section is highlighted via `IntersectionObserver` with multiple thresholds and a top-biased rootMargin so the highlight tracks the user's actual reading position. - Section collapsed state is lifted into `ContentTab` (keyed by RHF field id) so reordering doesn't desync. #### Form-level safety + UX - **Unsaved-changes guard** — `beforeunload` prompt while the form is dirty; **Publish** opens a "Publish without saving?" confirm dialog if clicked with local edits; **Cmd/Ctrl+S** triggers the same Save path as the button (validation, tab error routing, dirty tracking). - **Type-to-confirm delete** when the post is currently published — must type the slug to enable the destructive action. - **Tab error routing** — failed submits auto-switch to the first tab containing an invalid field; each tab shows a small red dot when it has errors. - **Searchable Combobox for Related Articles** (`cmdk`-based) — typeahead, grouped by "Same category" / "Other categories", with up/down reorder per row. Replaces the old 500-row `Select`. - **Preset Author/Reviewer Selects** reflect the current selection (or show "Custom: …" placeholder when fields don't match a preset). - **Slug regenerate** button next to the slug input (disabled once published, with explanatory tooltip). - **Live read-time hint** under the Read Time field — strips HTML, counts words across intro/summary/body1/body2/sections/subsections/FAQ answers, divides by 225 wpm; shown as a placeholder + helper text. - **Cascade-clear warning toast** when changing Category Page silently invalidates the current Category or Subcategory. #### Accessibility + interaction polish - All nested inputs (sections, subsections, FAQs, CTAs, related articles) have stable `id` + `htmlFor` pairing. - Bare-text "Sublabel"/"Link" labels in the CTAs tab wrapped in proper `LabelWithTip`. - Touch targets bumped from `h-7`/`h-8` (~28–32px) to `h-9` (36px) across all nested controls; icon buttons have `aria-label`s. #### Image handling - Image URL inputs (author, reviewer, main, thumbnail, OG, Twitter, CTA1/2, per-section image, per-subsection product image) render an inline 128×80 click-to-open thumbnail so editors can verify assets before saving; broken URLs show a "Failed to load" badge. - The disabled "Choose from Aperture" placeholder button has been **hidden** until Phase 3 ships a real custom picker (no embeddable Aperture picker exists; needs a custom modal hitting Aperture's REST API). #### Permissions + workflow - **Publish / Unpublish / Delete** buttons gated on `blog:publish` and `blog:delete`. - **Preview link** opens `/blog/preview/` in the webflow app for any draft. ### Webflow public site (`apps/webflow`) - **`/blog`, `/blog/[slug]`, `/blog-category/[category]`** now fetch from `getPublishedPosts` / `getPostBySlug` (server-side, ISR `revalidate: 60`). Static data import is gone. - **`/blog/preview/[token]`** — token-gated draft preview route, `noindex`, with a sticky "DRAFT PREVIEW" banner. Uses the same V7 layout as live posts so editors see exactly what will ship. - **Design preserved** — the V2/V7 redesign from `main` (`BlogListingV2`, `CategoryListingV2`, `BlogPostV7`) is consumed unchanged; `ApiBlogPost extends BlogPost` means the new components accept CMS data without modification. - **CMS-aware rendering** — `BlogPostV7` now also renders: - `section.subsections[]` — numbered subsections use the existing `.blog-v7-numbered-steps` styling; medication subsections render as product cards with image + title + body. - Aggregated FAQs — `parseBlogPost` flattens per-section `section.faqs[]` into the top-level FAQ accordion. - Aggregated references — `parseBlogPost` concatenates per-section `section.references` into a single string passed to V7's references accordion. - **Dynamic sitemap** + **`/robots.ts`** — both pull live published slugs from the API. - **Full SEO JSON-LD** — `Article`, `MedicalWebPage` (when reviewed), `FAQPage` (when sections contain FAQs), `BreadcrumbList`, `CollectionPage` for listings. - **API mapper** (`blog-api.ts`) — `mapPost` converts the backend payload to the shared `BlogPost` shape; tightened `sections` typing so `null` from the DB becomes `undefined`. ## Migration 1. Run `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate` to apply migrations `298` (blog_posts) and `299` (audit-log entity type). 2. Run the seed: `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend tsx scripts/seed-blog-posts.ts` to import the 47 legacy posts. 3. Set new env vars on backend and webflow (see `.env.example` diffs): - **Backend**: `APERTURE_API_URL`, `APERTURE_API_KEY`, `REVALIDATE_SECRET` - **Webflow**: `BACKEND_URL`, `REVALIDATE_SECRET` (must match backend) 4. After deploy, the static `blog-posts.ts` file is no longer read by routes but is left in place for the seed script + as the type source for `BlogPost` shared between admin and webflow. ## New dependencies - `apps/admin`: `@tiptap/react`, `@tiptap/starter-kit`, `@tiptap/extension-link`, `@tiptap/extension-superscript`, `@tiptap/extension-placeholder`, `@tiptap/pm` (all pinned to `^3.22.4`). ## Known follow-ups (left as branch TODOs) 1. **Author/reviewer fields not threaded to the public site yet.** `authorImage`, `reviewerImage`, `reviewerTitle`, `reviewerBio` are stored in the DB and editable in the admin, but `apps/webflow/src/lib/blog-api.ts → mapPost()` doesn't pass them through — V7's doctor profile renders fallback values until 4 lines are added to the mapper. 2. **`featuredOrder` / `globalFeatured` not implemented.** `BlogListingV2` reads these to curate the hero + side-card slots; right now everything falls back to `featured` / first-post. Needs a migration adding `featured_order INT` + `global_featured BOOLEAN`, repo column lists, admin schema/UI, and `mapPost` mapping. 3. **Sticky editor header.** The post title / status badge / Preview / Delete / Save row + tabs scroll out of view in long posts. Cmd+S landed first as the lower-cost alternative; making the header sticky is a layout-only follow-up. 4. **Live side-by-side preview pane.** Editors currently round-trip through Save → Preview → new tab. With TipTap providing in-editor WYSIWYG this is less critical, but a debounced render of `BlogPostV7` from form state would close the loop. 5. **Aperture picker (Phase 3).** The "Choose from Aperture" button is hidden because no embeddable Aperture picker exists. Needs a custom modal that hits Aperture's REST API (`apt_xxx` Bearer auth via the existing backend proxy) and returns `cdn_url`. ## Test plan - [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend test` — blog repo + service + audit specs pass. - [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/webflow exec tsc --noEmit` — webflow types clean. - [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/admin exec tsc --noEmit` — admin types clean. - [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/admin build` — admin production build succeeds. - [ ] Apply migrations + seed; visit `/blog` on webflow → 47 posts render with V2 design. - [ ] Open a post in admin, edit a field, save → updated copy appears on `/blog/` within 60 s (ISR). - [ ] Create a draft post in admin → click "Preview" → V7 layout renders with the draft banner; not indexable. - [ ] Publish the draft → public `/blog/` returns it after revalidation. - [ ] Unpublish a post → public route 404s after revalidation; admin still sees it. - [ ] Audit log shows entries for create / update / publish / unpublish / delete with correct actor + diff. - [ ] Editor image fields show thumbnails for valid URLs and a "Failed to load" badge for broken ones. - [ ] CMS-authored sections with `subsections` (numbered + medication), per-section `faqs`, and per-section `references` all render correctly on `/blog/` and `/blog/preview/`. - [ ] **Editor: rich text round-trip** — open one of the seeded legacy posts, confirm Summary/Introduction/section bodies/FAQ answers render in the TipTap editor with their original formatting (paragraphs, lists, links, `` reference numbers); save and verify HTML output still renders identically on `/blog/`. - [ ] **Editor: reorder** — use up/down buttons on sections, subsections, FAQs, and related articles; verify the order persists after Save and renders in the new order on the public page. - [ ] **Editor: sticky TOC** — at `lg+` width, the right-rail TOC lists every section heading, scrolls them into view on click (auto-expanding if collapsed), and highlights the section currently in the viewport. - [ ] **Editor: unsaved-changes guard** — make a local edit, attempt to navigate away → browser confirm appears; click Publish with unsaved edits → "Publish without saving?" dialog appears. - [ ] **Editor: Cmd/Ctrl+S** — saves the post and runs the same validation/tab-routing as the Save button. - [ ] **Editor: type-to-confirm delete** — Delete on a published post requires typing the slug to enable the destructive action. - [ ] **Editor: tab error routing** — submit with an invalid required field on a non-active tab → tab gets a red dot and the editor switches to it automatically. - [ ] **Editor: searchable Related Articles** — typeahead filters by title; "Same category" group appears first; Cmd/Ctrl-click on the trigger opens. - [ ] **Editor: slug regenerate** — Regenerate button rebuilds the slug from the title for drafts; disabled once the post is published. - [ ] **Editor: live read-time** — typing into body fields updates the "Auto-estimate: N min" hint under Read Time. ## Migration collision note Resolved during the merge from `main`: blog migrations were renumbered from `296` / `297` to `298` / `299` because `main` introduced `296_audit_log_product_variant_entity_type.sql` after this branch was cut.

Blog content was hard-coded in `apps/webflow/src/data

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/wl hl forms migration
GoRocky/core.description:
# Port WL / HL form templates and custom components to `apps/web`

Brings the GoSlim (weight loss), GoFuller (hair loss), WL trial pack, and
free-consultation form flows from `apps/goforms` into the core patient-facing
app (`apps/web`), so the existing form templates in the backend render with
the right custom components and themes.

## Summary

- **New custom component groups** for `goslim`, `gofuller`, `wl-trial`, and
  `free-consultation` flows, backed by a new shared scaffolding layer
  (`FormLayoutCentered`, `FormLayoutWithImage`, animations, `GoSlimHeader`,
  `NavigationButtons`, `ProgressBar`, `goslim-2/*` primitives,
  `useKeyboardShortcuts`).
- **Form renderer improvements** — theme routing, validation, required-field
  gating, label/placeholder rendering, section layout, multi-select
  exclusive behavior, and radio-button styling.
- **Backend fixes + 3 migrations** to match the new frontend wiring:
  extracting `first_name`/`last_name` into the lead, and adjusting template
  metadata so the WL/HL forms actually submit cleanly in core.

## What changed

### 1. Backend

**`fix(leads)`: first_name / last_name extraction** (`48807e3e4`)
- `FormAnswerExtractionService.FIELD_MAPPINGS` was missing entries for
  `first_name` / `last_name`, so those columns were only populated during
  initial lead creation — never on subsequent enrichment passes.
- Added both with sensible aliases (`firstName`, `givenName`, `lastName`,
  `familyName`, `surname`).

**`feat(forms)`: 3 form-template migrations** (`9026c831f`)
- `296_unconditional_medical_interventions.sql` — strip
  `is_conditional` / `condition_rules` from `medical_interventions` so it
  always renders regardless of selected conditions.
- `297_optional_installment_product_fields.sql` — flip
  `is_required = false` on six hidden installment fields
  (`product_package_option`, `product_id`, `product_sku`, `product_name`,
  `product_price`, `number_of_installments`). The backing components are
  stubbed as `AutoSubmit` in core, so nothing fills these at submit time.
  Previously the tiktok form failed with `400 Bad Request` on submit.
- `298_trial_assessment_landing_component.sql` — point
  `gorocky-wl-trial-assessment`'s landing section at `WLTrialLandingPage`
  instead of the inherited `GoSlimLandingPage`.
- All three ship with matching rollbacks.

### 2. Frontend — renderer / validation / theming (`d1b609fd5`)

**Theming** (`apps/web/src/lib/forms/theme-config.ts`)
- `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2`, `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2-tiktok`, and
  `gorocky-wl-trial-assessment` now map to `edTheme` (rust/orange) instead
  of the yellow `goslimTheme`.

**Continue-button gating** (`useFormController.ts`, `form-view-types.ts`,
`LegacyFormView.tsx`)
- New memoized `isCurrentSectionComplete` flag — the Continue button stays
  disabled until every visible required question has a non-empty answer.
- Exposed on the `FormController` interface and wired into the Continue
  button's `disabled` prop.

**Question rendering** (`QuestionRenderer.tsx`)
- Short-text questions no longer use a floating label embedded inside the
  input — they now render a proper label above the field like every other
  question type (fixes the “Please enter your email address...” text
  rendering *inside* the input box).
- `number` / `decimal` inputs now forward `question.metadata.placeholder`
  (so body-measurement fields show `170`, `70`, `40`, `36` as placeholders).

**Section layout** (`LegacyFormView.tsx`, `SectionHeader.tsx`)
- Stop rendering `section.description` under the title in both legacy and
  section-header paths (removes duplicative subtitles like
  "Medications you take" under "Current Medications").
- Add top padding (`pt-8 md:pt-12`) to section headers so titles have
  breathing room under the progress bar.

**Validation builder** (`lib/forms/form-builder.ts`)
- `question.validation` is stored in the DB as an array of rules
  (`[{type: "pattern", value, message}]`), but the Zod builder only read
  flat object keys (`validation.pattern`, `validation.minLength`, …), so
  array-form validation was silently ignored — the GoFuller phone-number
  pattern (and anything similar) never ran.
- Now normalizes array rules into the flat shape the builder already
  understands (`pattern`, `minLength`, `maxLength`, `min`, `max`, and
  `email`).

**Fields** (`RadioButtonGroup.tsx`, `MultiSelectWithExtras.tsx`)
- Radio group: selected option description color switched from
  hardcoded `text-blue-700` to `text-gray-700` so it matches any theme.
- Multi-select: auto-mark `"None of the above"`-style options (value
  `none`/`none_of_the_above` or matching label) as *exclusive* — selecting
  them clears other picks, and selecting any other option clears them.
  No DB migration required.

### 3. Frontend — custom components

**Shared scaffolding and layouts** (`f11a65c8d`, `157b8bb58`,
`5924247e8`)
- Page wrappers: `FormLayoutCentered`, `FormLayoutWithImage`.
- Shared atoms: `GoSlimHeader`, `NavigationButtons`, `ProgressBar`.
- `animations/`: `AnimatedButton`, `AnimatedContentSection`,
  `AnimatedFormField`, `AnimatedIcon`, `AnimatedList`,
  `AnimatedPageContainer`.
- `goslim-2/`: `BadgedTitle`, `ChoiceButton`, `InputField`, `Navbar`.
- `useKeyboardShortcuts` hook.
- `GoRockyLogo` / `GoSlimLogo` brand components + new image assets in
  `public/images/`.

**Goslim WL assessment** (`5924247e8`, `850636ce8`)
- New: `GoSlimConsentPage`, `GoSlimCannotPrescribe`,
  `GoSlimClearToMeetDoctor`, `GoSlimPersonalDetailsPage` (rebuilt with
  image layout + theme-driven focus colors), `GoSlimTikTokLandingPage`
  (rebuilt with image layout + animated benefits list), plus
  `GoSlimLandingPage` updates.
- All registered through `goslim/index.ts`.

**GoFuller (HL)** (`e3c4fee49`)
- `HLWelcomePage`, `HLHairLossScale` (+ mirrored tweak in
  `apps/goforms`).
- New `gofuller/index.ts` registers them.

**WL trial** (`7c0e98e08`, `5924247e8`)
- `WLTrialLandingPage`, `WLTrialConsentPage`, `WLTrialNotAvailable`, and
  `WLTrialPersonalDetailsPage` (theme-driven focus colors).
- New `wl-trial/index.ts` registers everything.

**Free consultation** (`d48fe529f`)
- 22-page flow: landing → goals → motivation → personal details → date
  of birth → body-measurements intro → height / weight / waist / hip →
  BMI result → health background → medical history / family medical
  history → current medications → previous surgeries → allergies →
  nicotine usage → GLP-1 experience → completion.

**Registry + renderer wiring** (`555fadfda`)
- Top-level `custom/index.ts` registers the four new component groups.
- `FormRendererCore` tweaks to match the new layouts.
- `form-fixtures.ts` updated to cover the new slugs.

**Other** (`5924247e8`)
- `AutoSubmit` now surfaces submission errors via `console.error` and
  clears `submitted.current` on failure so a retry is possible (used
  while debugging the tiktok 400).

### 4. UI primitives (`d4075485b`)

- `Input` / `Switch` tweaks to support theme-driven focus and active
  colors used by the new forms.
- `date-picker-spinner`: new wheel-style date picker used by the free
  consultation date-of-birth step.

## Migrations

Run after pulling:

```bash
pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate
```

Applies:
- `296_unconditional_medical_interventions.sql`
- `297_optional_installment_product_fields.sql`
- `298_trial_assessment_landing_component.sql`

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate` applies cleanly; rollbacks
      restore prior state
- [ ] Walk `/forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2` end to end — ed-theme colors,
      no yellow focus rings, image layout on personal details, Continue
      button disabled until required fields filled, submits and redirects
      to `/products/wm`
- [ ] Walk `/forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2-tiktok` end to end — tiktok
      landing with image, submits and redirects to `/products/wm`
- [ ] Walk `/forms/gorocky-wl-trial-assessment` — `WLTrialLandingPage`
      shows (not `GoSlimLandingPage`)
- [ ] Walk `/forms/gofuller-assessment` — phone-number field rejects
      invalid PH numbers and shows the pattern error message
- [ ] On the `medical_conditions` question, selecting "None of the above"
      clears other selections, and picking another option clears
      "None of the above"
- [ ] Body measurements show `170`, `70`, `40`, `36` placeholders
- [ ] Submit a form with first + last name — verify both persist to
      `leads.first_name` and `leads.last_name`

## Files changed

High-level directories touched:

- `apps/backend/src/modules/leads/services/form-answer-extraction.service.ts`
- `apps/backend/src/database/migrations/{296,297,298}*.sql` (+ rollbacks)
- `apps/web/src/lib/forms/{theme-config,form-builder}.ts`
- `apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/*`
- `apps/web/src/components/forms/fields/{RadioButtonGroup,MultiSelectWithExtras}.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/{goslim,gofuller,wl-trial,free-consultation,shared}/*`
- `apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/{FormLayoutCentered,FormLayoutWithImage}.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/{GoRockyLogo,GoSlimLogo}.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/ui/{input,switch,date-picker-spinner}.tsx`
- `apps/web/public/{goslim.svg,images/goslim-*.png}`
- `apps/goforms/src/components/forms/custom/gofuller/custom-form-pages/HLHairLossScale.tsx`
GoRocky/core.summary: **New custom component groups** for `goslim`, `gofuller`, `wl-trial`, and `free-consultation` flows, backed by a new shared scaffolding layer (`FormLayoutCentered`, `FormLayoutWithImage`, animations, `GoSlimHeader`, `NavigationButtons`, `ProgressBar`, `goslim-2/*` primitives, `useKeyboardShortcuts`)

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/reordering flow
GoRocky/core.description: # PR: Form Submission Expiry & Reorder Confirmation Flow ## Summary Implements a 90-day form submission expiry system for the ED assessment. Returning users who reorder within 3 months can confirm their medical info is still accurate instead of re-filling the form. After expiry, they must complete the form again. All confirmations and submissions are logged with timestamps for audit. ## What's New ### Backend: Submission Expiry Tracking - Added `effective_expires_at` column to `form_submissions` table - Created `form_submission_confirmations` audit table (tracks who confirmed, when, previous/new expiry dates, source) - Expiry is computed at submission time from `category_staleness_config` (90 days for ED) - Case-insensitive category matching in submission queries ### Backend: Reorder & Confirmation Endpoints - `GET /forms/submissions/reorder-summary?category=ED` — returns latest submission with answers for the review page - `POST /forms/submissions/:id/confirm` — patient confirms answers are still accurate, extends expiry by 90 days - `GET /admin/users/:id/form-submissions` — admin view of all submissions with expiry/validity status - `GET /admin/users/:id/form-submissions/:submissionId/confirmations` — confirmation audit history ### Admin: Forms Tab on User Detail Page - New "Forms" tab under `/users/[id]` showing all form submissions - Displays: form name, category, submitted date, expires date, validity status (Valid/Expired/Unknown), days remaining - Expandable drawer with confirmation history and ability to manually confirm ### Frontend: Reorder Review Page (`/dashboard/reorder/review`) - Shows medical info summary cards from the user's latest submission (age, conditions, medications, nitrates) - Checkbox confirmation: "I confirm that the information above is accurate..." - Two CTAs: "My Information Is Still Accurate" (confirms + redirects to product) and "Update My Information" (redirects to form) - Answer summary transformer maps v2 form question keys to human-readable labels ### Frontend: ED Assessment V2 Improvements - Moved contact details section to end of form (was position 3, now position 10) - Removed summary section (EDSummarySection) - Fixed section display order (ed_duration before previous_treatment) - Marked "None of the above" and "Not taking any medicine" as exclusive options (deselects others) - Fixed submit button not showing loading state (added `requestAnimationFrame` + moved `setIsSubmitting(false)` to catch-only) - Disqualification for nitrates now triggers before contact details section (not after submission) ## Migrations | # | Name | Description | |---|------|-------------| | 241 | `form_submission_expiry_confirmations` | Adds `effective_expires_at` column, creates confirmations table, updates ED staleness to 90 days, backfills existing submissions | | 253 | `move_contact_details_to_end` | Explicit display_order for all sections, removes summary, moves contact_details to position 10 | | 254 | `exclusive_none_options` | Marks "none" options as exclusive in medical_conditions and current_medications | | 255 | `backfill_ed_v2_expiry` | Sets v2 template category to `'ed'`, backfills `effective_expires_at` for existing v2 submissions | ## Key Files Changed **Backend:** - `modules/forms/repositories/submission.repository.ts` — expiry queries, confirmation logic, reorder summary - `modules/forms/forms.controller.ts` — reorder-summary and confirm endpoints - `modules/forms/services/forms.service.ts` — expiry computation on submission - `modules/admin-users/admin-users.controller.ts` — admin form submission endpoints **Frontend:** - `app/(dashboard)/dashboard/reorder/review/page.tsx` — reorder review page - `hooks/use-reorder-review.ts` — React Query hooks for reorder flow - `lib/reorder/answer-summary.ts` — transforms form answers to summary cards - `lib/api/forms.ts` — API client for reorder summary and confirmation - `components/forms/renderer/FormRendererCore.tsx` — disqualification check before contact_details - `components/forms/custom/ed/NitratesCheckSection.tsx` — continues through form (no immediate redirect) - `components/forms/custom/ed/GoRockyContactDetails.tsx` — fixed submit button loading state **Admin:** - `components/users/UserFormsTab.tsx` — forms tab with expiry and confirmation UI ## How It Works 1. User submits ED assessment → `effective_expires_at` set to NOW + 90 days 2. User clicks "Buy Again" on dashboard → routed to `/dashboard/reorder/review` 3. Review page fetches latest submission via `GET /forms/submissions/reorder-summary` 4. User reviews summary cards and either: - **Confirms** → `POST /forms/submissions/:id/confirm` extends expiry, redirects to product page - **Updates** → redirects to form to fill out again (creates new submission) 5. If no valid submission exists (expired or none) → redirected to form 6. Admin can view all submissions, expiry status, and confirmation history under user details - Added Forms tab under Users page - Upon clicking "Buy Again" CTA, user will be redirected to reordering flow - Upon clicking "Update My Information" CTA, users will be redirected to the ed form but with their previous form's answers pre-filled out - If they click on the "My Information is Still Accurate" CTA, it should update in the backend that the user confirmed their last submission is still accurate and redirect the user to the product page of the product they purchased last)

All confirmations and submissions are logged with tim

GoRocky/core.title: Fix/product page redesign
GoRocky/core.description: # ED Product Page Redesign ## Summary - Redesigned webapp product page to match layout of webflow product page - Redesigned ProductCard layout with consistent vertical alignment across cards using flexbox - Added reusable pre-qualified banner component shown to users who completed the ED assessment - Added sold count display inline with reviews on product cards - Populated Tadalafil Strips with rating, review count, and sold count data ## Changes ### New Component - **`pre-qualified-banner.tsx`** — Reusable banner with customizable title/description, uses brand color CSS variables with fallbacks ### ProductCard (`ProductCard.tsx`) - Card uses `flex flex-col` so content sections align across cards of different heights - Key benefits section set to `flex-1` to push quantity selector, price, CTA, and reviews to the same vertical position regardless of content length - Added "Recommended" badge (top-left corner) when `isRecommended` is true - GoRocky/core.title: Feature/reordering flow
GoRocky/core.description: # PR: Form Submission Expiry & Reorder Confirmation Flow ## Summary Implements a 90-day form submission expiry system for the ED assessment. Returning users who reorder within 3 months can confirm their medical info is still accurate instead of re-filling the form. After expiry, they must complete the form again. All confirmations and submissions are logged with timestamps for audit. ## What's New ### Backend: Submission Expiry Tracking - Added `effective_expires_at` column to `form_submissions` table - Created `form_submission_confirmations` audit table (tracks who confirmed, when, previous/new expiry dates, source) - Expiry is computed at submission time from `category_staleness_config` (90 days for ED) - Case-insensitive category matching in submission queries ### Backend: Reorder & Confirmation Endpoints - `GET /forms/submissions/reorder-summary?category=ED` — returns latest submission with answers for the review page - `POST /forms/submissions/:id/confirm` — patient confirms answers are still accurate, extends expiry by 90 days - `GET /admin/users/:id/form-submissions` — admin view of all submissions with expiry/validity status - `GET /admin/users/:id/form-submissions/:submissionId/confirmations` — confirmation audit history ### Admin: Forms Tab on User Detail Page - New "Forms" tab under `/users/[id]` showing all form submissions - Displays: form name, category, submitted date, expires date, validity status (Valid/Expired/Unknown), days remaining - Expandable drawer with confirmation history and ability to manually confirm ### Frontend: Reorder Review Page (`/dashboard/reorder/review`) - Shows medical info summary cards from the user's latest submission (age, conditions, medications, nitrates) - Checkbox confirmation: "I confirm that the information above is accurate..." - Two CTAs: "My Information Is Still Accurate" (confirms + redirects to product) and "Update My Information" (redirects to form) - Answer summary transformer maps v2 form question keys to human-readable labels ### Frontend: ED Assessment V2 Improvements - Moved contact details section to end of form (was position 3, now position 10) - Removed summary section (EDSummarySection) - Fixed section display order (ed_duration before previous_treatment) - Marked "None of the above" and "Not taking any medicine" as exclusive options (deselects others) - Fixed submit button not showing loading state (added `requestAnimationFrame` + moved `setIsSubmitting(false)` to catch-only) - Disqualification for nitrates now triggers before contact details section (not after submission) ## Migrations | # | Name | Description | |---|------|-------------| | 241 | `form_submission_expiry_confirmations` | Adds `effective_expires_at` column, creates confirmations table, updates ED staleness to 90 days, backfills existing submissions | | 253 | `move_contact_details_to_end` | Explicit display_order for all sections, removes summary, moves contact_details to position 10 | | 254 | `exclusive_none_options` | Marks "none" options as exclusive in medical_conditions and current_medications | | 255 | `backfill_ed_v2_expiry` | Sets v2 template category to `'ed'`, backfills `effective_expires_at` for existing v2 submissions | ## Key Files Changed **Backend:** - `modules/forms/repositories/submission.repository.ts` — expiry queries, confirmation logic, reorder summary - `modules/forms/forms.controller.ts` — reorder-summary and confirm endpoints - `modules/forms/services/forms.service.ts` — expiry computation on submission - `modules/admin-users/admin-users.controller.ts` — admin form submission endpoints **Frontend:** - `app/(dashboard)/dashboard/reorder/review/page.tsx` — reorder review page - `hooks/use-reorder-review.ts` — React Query hooks for reorder flow - `lib/reorder/answer-summary.ts` — transforms form answers to summary cards - `lib/api/forms.ts` — API client for reorder summary and confirmation - `components/forms/renderer/FormRendererCore.tsx` — disqualification check before contact_details - `components/forms/custom/ed/NitratesCheckSection.tsx` — continues through form (no immediate redirect) - `components/forms/custom/ed/GoRockyContactDetails.tsx` — fixed submit button loading state **Admin:** - `components/users/UserFormsTab.tsx` — forms tab with expiry and confirmation UI ## How It Works 1. User submits ED assessment → `effective_expires_at` set to NOW + 90 days 2. User clicks "Buy Again" on dashboard → routed to `/dashboard/reorder/review` 3. Review page fetches latest submission via `GET /forms/submissions/reorder-summary` 4. User reviews summary cards and either: - **Confirms** → `POST /forms/submissions/:id/confirm` extends expiry, redirects to product page - **Updates** → redirects to form to fill out again (creates new submission) 5. If no valid submission exists (expired or none) → redirected to form 6. Admin can view all submissions, expiry status, and confirmation history under user details - Added Forms tab under Users page - Upon clicking "Buy Again" CTA, user will be redirected to reordering flow - Upon clicking "Update My Information" CTA, users will be redirected to the ed form but with their previous form's answers pre-filled out
GoRocky/core.summary: Implements a 90-day form submission expiry system for the ED assessment. Returning users who reorder within 3 months can confirm their medical info is still accurate instead of re-filling the form. After expiry, they must complete the form again. All confirmations and submissions are logged with tim

GoRocky/goforms.title: Chore/trial pack tweaks
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# PR: Trial Pack Tweaks

## Summary

Minor refinements to the WL Trial Pack assessment flow — copy updates, branding fixes, and removing an unnecessary form section.

## Changes

- **Remove treatment preferences section**: Added migration to drop the `treatment-preferences` section (and its `treatment_affordability` question) from `gorocky-wl-trial-assessment`, since it's not relevant to the trial pack flow
- **Update trial pack pricing text**: Changed benefit text from "One-time payment for your first shot" to "Only ₱2,500 for your first shot"
- **Update consent page image**: Replaced consent page image with new asset
- **Update product name and nurse visit text**: Removed "Metro Manila only" restriction from the nurse visit banner text, updated trial pack product naming
- **Fix input focus colors**: Updated personal details form input focus ring colors from blue to ED 2026 color variable for brand consistency

## Files Changed

- 6 files changed, +127 / -9 lines
- 3 GoSlim WL Trial component updates (LandingPage, ConsentPage, PersonalDetailsPage)
- 1 WLTrialPackSelection update
- 2 new SQL migration files (+ rollback)

## Test Plan

- [ ] Verify the treatment preferences question no longer appears in the `gorocky-wl-trial-assessment` form
- [ ] Verify updated pricing text ("Only ₱2,500") shows on the landing page
- [ ] Verify consent page renders with the new image
- [ ] Verify personal details input fields use correct focus ring color (not blue)
- [ ] Verify nurse visit text no longer says "Metro Manila only"
- [ ] Run migration and rollback successfully
GoRocky/goforms.summary: Minor refinements to the WL Trial Pack assessment flow — copy updates, branding fixes, and removing an unnecessary form section.

- **New subscription + add-on hybrid payment flow** — Created a new API endpoint (`/api/payments/subscription/create`) that handles subscriptions with one-time add-on products (e.g., Finoxidil Spray), with separate first vs. recurring payment amounts

- **CSRF protection for subscription payments** — Added CSRF token validation and server-side price verification on the new subscription endpoint
- **Pricing updates** — Updated Finasteride monthly subscription from ₱499 → ₱549, corrected Shopify variant IDs
- **Phone number validation improvements** — Real-time input formatting, leading zero removal, 10-digit max enforcement
- **ESLint/build fixes** — Removed unused variables, replaced `any` types with `unknown`, added missing `useCallback` dependency

## Key Files Changed
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `checkout/page.tsx` | Major update (+499/-69 lines) for add-on product support |
| `subscription/create/route.ts` | New 186-line subscription API endpoint |
| `resolve-base-url.ts` | New shared payment URL utility |
| `HLPlanSelection.tsx` | Plan selection UI updates |
| `HLTierInclusions.tsx` | Tier inclusion updates |
| `getRecommendation.ts` | Pricing & variant ID fixes |

## Test Plan

GoRocky/goforms.title: Feature/ph phone normalization
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# Standardize PH phone numbers to E.164 format

Introduces a single normalizer (`normalizePhMobileToE164`) that converts all Philippine mobile number variants (`09XX`, `9XX`, `639XX`, `+639XX`) to canonical E.164 format (`+639XXXXXXXXX`) across the entire stack — client inputs, API routes, checkout flows, and database.

## What changed

### Core utility

- New `normalizePhMobileToE164()` function in `src/lib/validation/ph-phone.ts` with 25 unit tests
- Accepts `09XXXXXXXXX`, `9XXXXXXXXX`, `639XXXXXXXXX`, `+639XXXXXXXXX` (with optional spaces/dashes/parens)
- Throws descriptive errors for invalid input; idempotent on already-normalized values

### Client-side phone inputs

- Moved phone normalization from `onChange` (every keystroke) to `onBlur` across all form components — prevents jarring mid-typing value changes
- Fixed FreeConsultationPersonalDetailsPage: `onChange` was normalizing to `+639...` which displayed next to the `+63` UI prefix, making it look like `+64...`. Now strips `+63` on blur for correct display
- Updated validation from regex `pattern` to normalizer-based `validate` for consistent error messages
- Affected: GoSlimPersonalDetailsPage, GoRockyContactDetails, KindredPersonalDetailsPage, FreeConsultationPersonalDetailsPage, ContactCard (quiz)

### Server-side API routes

- All submit and payment routes now normalize phone via `normalizePhMobileToE164()` before storing/forwarding
- Phone is now **optional** (was changed to required, which broke affiliate forms that don't collect phone). Falls back gracefully to `null`
- Updated rate limiting to fall back to email/sessionId when phone not available
- Affected: `submit-secure`, `submit`, `create-plan`, `init-customer`, `v3/create-payment-link`

### Checkout pages

- GoFuller: removed duplicate phone input block left behind from merge, uses normalizer for validation
- GoSlim: replaced ad-hoc `+63` prefixing (which produced invalid `+6309...`) with `normalizePhMobileToE164()`
- Installment order summaries (GoSlim, Kindred) and GoSlimPackagesPage use normalizer before calling payment APIs

### Database migrations (run in order: 037 → 038 → 039)

- **037**: Standardizes `form_questions` validation rules for all `phone_number` questions to accept all PH mobile variants
- **038**: Backfills existing `customers.phone` and `form_submissions.submitted_phone` to E.164. Creates backup tables, detects collisions/invalid numbers, aborts if issues found
- **039**: Drops `NOT NULL` on phone columns (for phoneless forms like affiliates), cleans up empty/placeholder values, adds `CHECK (phone ~ '^\+639\d{9}$')` constraints

All three migrations have corresponding rollback scripts in `supabase/rollbacks/production-forms/`.

## Files changed

| Area | Files | Count |
|------|-------|-------|
| Core utility | `ph-phone.ts`, `ph-phone.test.ts`, `package.json` | 3 |
| Migrations & rollbacks | `037_*`, `038_*`, `039_*` + rollbacks | 6 |
| API routes | `submit-secure`, `submit`, `create-plan`, `init-customer`, `v3/create-payment-link` | 5 |
| Form components | GoSlim, GoRocky, Kindred personal details + ContactCard + contact schema | 6 |
| Checkout pages | GoFuller, GoSlim checkouts + installment summaries + packages | 5 |
| Other | `.gitignore` | 1 |

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run contract:ph-phone` — 25 unit tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
- [ ] Test phone input on GoSlim, GoRocky ED, Free Consultation forms — verify normalize-on-blur UX
- [ ] Test FreeConsultation: entering `09171234567` should display `9171234567` next to `+63` prefix after blur
- [ ] Test checkout flows (GoSlim, GoFuller) — verify no 400 errors on submit
- [ ] Test affiliate forms — verify submission works without phone field
- [ ] Run migrations 037 → 038 → 039 on staging, verify backfill and constraints
- [ ] Verify rollback scripts work: 039 → 038 → 037
GoRocky/goforms.summary: Introduces a single normalizer (`normalizePhMobileToE164`) that converts all Philippine mobile number variants (`09XX`, `9XX`, `639XX`, `+639XX`) to canonical E.164 format (`+639XXXXXXXXX`) across the entire stack — client inputs, API routes, checkout flows, and database. - New `normalizePhMobileToE

GoRocky/goforms.title: Fix/goforms hl subscriptions v1 feedback
GoRocky/goforms.description:
- Fixed recommendation logic to follow:

“Which statement sounds most like you?”
A. I want the strongest, most aggressive regrowth plan available.
 B. I want something simple and easy to stay consistent with.
 C. I want the most affordable option that still works.
 D. I prefer to avoid certain medications and keep it safer/minimal.
 E. I’m not sure — recommend what works best for my case.

**New Recommendation**
A and Male -> Finoxidil Capsule
Female -> Minoxidil Capsule (Disregarding whatever options they choose as long as the gender/sex is FEMALE the only options we can provide is Minoxidil)
B or C or E and Male -> Finasteride 5mg (2.5mg)
D and Male -> Minoxidil Capsule
GoRocky/goforms.summary: - Fixed recommendation logic to follow: “Which statement sounds most like you?” A. I want the strongest, most aggressive regrowth plan available.

GoRocky/erp-ui.title: fix: hard-filter doctor assignment and trigger re-assignment on risk change
GoRocky/erp-ui.description:
## Summary
- **Hard-filter in `findOptimalDoctor`**: Doctors without `assignment_rules` or whose `risk_levels` don't include the order's risk level are now excluded entirely. Previously, risk level was a -10 priority boost that could still allow mismatched assignments.
- **Trigger re-assignment on risk level change**: After a manual risk assessment update, if the risk level changed, fires a request to the backend's new `reassign-doctors-on-risk-change` endpoint to re-assign doctors on pending orders.

### Files changed
- `src/modules/doctors/services/doctorService.ts` — `findOptimalDoctor` now hard-filters by risk level instead of soft priority boost
- `src/modules/risk-assessment/services/riskAssessmentService.ts` — triggers doctor re-assignment after manual review when risk level changes

## Test plan
- [x] Verify doctor assignment on the UI only shows doctors matching the order's risk level
- [x] Manually change a customer's risk level → confirm backend re-assignment endpoint is called
- [x] Confirm fire-and-forget: risk assessment update succeeds even if re-assignment call fails
GoRocky/erp-ui.summary: **Hard-filter in `findOptimalDoctor`**: Doctors without `assignment_rules` or whose `risk_levels` don't include the order's risk level are now excluded entirely. GoRocky/core.description: # Home page tweaks + design system polish ## Summary - New `/web` **404 page** ported from `/webflow`. - **Sign-in flow** action buttons migrated to the design system's gradient primary + bordered secondary `Button` from `@gorocky/goforms-ds`. - Header drawer's **"Create an account"** button uses the secondary DS button. - **`NewsletterSection`** and **`HeroSection`** on the home page converted from inline `style={{}}` to Tailwind; dead `.gr-newsletter-*` / `.gr-hero-*` rules removed from `globals.css`. - **Webflow `HeroSection`** marquee ticker restored (was never ported when this file was copied from `/web`). - Hero subtitle line break is now desktop-only. --- ## Changes ### 1. New `/web` 404 page Ported [`apps/webflow/src/app/not-found.tsx`](apps/webflow/src/app/not-found.tsx) → [`apps/web/src/app/not-found.tsx`](apps/web/src/app/not-found.tsx). Uses `/web`'s own `Header` / `Footer` (auth-aware) and inlines the `CategoryCard` data since it's the only consumer. Treatment cards link to `/treatments/{gorocky,goslim,gofuller}` (routes already exist). **Files** - `apps/web/src/app/not-found.tsx` *(new)* 📷 *Screenshot: `/web` 404 — desktop + mobile* --- ### 2. Sign-in flow: gradient + secondary DS buttons Every action button in [`apps/web/src/app/auth/signin-flow/page.tsx`](apps/web/src/app/auth/signin-flow/page.tsx) replaced with `Button` from `@gorocky/goforms-ds`. | View | Primary (gradient) | Secondary (bordered) | |---|---|---| | email-entry | Continue | I have a password | | email-otp | Continue | Use SMS OTP | | password | Continue | — | | phone-entry | Send SMS | — | | phone-otp | Continue | Use Email OTP | Per-flow loading copy ("Verifying...", "Signing in...", "Sending...") is preserved as `children` with the existing `Loader2` icon — we use `disabled` rather than the DS `loading` prop, which would replace it with a generic "Submitting…". **Files** - `apps/web/src/app/auth/signin-flow/page.tsx` --- ### 3. Drawer "Create an account" → DS secondary button [`apps/web/src/components/layout/DrawerAccountSection.tsx`](apps/web/src/components/layout/DrawerAccountSection.tsx) — replaced the styled `` with `Button variant="secondary"`. Navigation moved into `onClick` using the already-in-scope `router.push`. **Trade-off:** element changes from `` → `
