GoRocky/core.title: Refactor/ed order xp v2 assets
GoRocky/core.description:
# refactor/ed-order-xp-v2-assets — PR summary Branch: `refactor/ed-order-xp-v2-assets` → `main` Polish pass on the new ED order experience: a focused checkout-style nav, several UX upgrades on the product detail page, expanded gallery assets, and proper out-of-stock handling across the catalog + PDP (Cialis flipped to OOS). ## Summary - **Order pages get their own minimal nav.** `/order/gorocky` and `/order/gorocky/[slug]` now render a centered, logo-only, non-sticky `OrderHeader` instead of the full site nav — fewer distractions during purchase intent. Every other surface still ships the regular `Header`. - **ED PDP polish.** Taller "More than medication" lifestyle image, sticky-hover feature cards that retain the last hovered image, swap the text `→` for a real Phosphor icon, and a horizontally scrollable gallery thumbnail strip with dynamic edge-fade masks that signal "more content offscreen". - **Galleries get more variety.** Four shared lifestyle images added across every ED product gallery (Sildenafil, Viagra, Tadalafil, Cialis — including per-dosage variants). Apps/web mirror also adjusted to use the unified "Ongoing support" feature-card image. - **Out-of-stock support exists for the new design.** The previous new-design surfaces had no OOS awareness — brand cards were hardcoded, the PDP didn't read `outOfStock`, and the sticky checkout bar would happily show a Buy CTA on an unavailable product. This PR closes those gaps and flips Cialis to OOS. ## Changes by area ### 1. `OrderHeader` for the order flow — `51046d552` | File | Change | | --- | --- | | `apps/webflow/src/components/layout/OrderHeader.tsx` | New. Logo-only, centered, non-sticky, 72px tall. | | `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/LegacyGorockyOrderPage.tsx` | Swap `Header` → `OrderHeader`. | | `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/NewGorockyOrderPage.tsx` | Swap `Header` → `OrderHeader`. | | `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/[slug]/page.tsx` | Swap `Header` → `OrderHeader`. | ### 2. ED detail page polish — `919ed019a` All in `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/[slug]/ProductDetailsClient.tsx`: - **Taller hero image.** "More than medication" wrapper: `aspect-square` → `aspect-[4/5]` on mobile, `md:h-[440px]` → `md:h-[560px]` on desktop. Right-side feature-card column stretches with it (`items-stretch` row). - **Sticky-hover feature cards.** Collapsed `hoveredFeature` + `pinnedFeature` into a single `activeFeature` state defaulting to `0`. Hover *sets* without resetting on `mouseLeave`; click also sets. Removed the unreachable `MORE_THAN_MEDICATION_IMAGE` fallback (its URL was identical to `FEATURE_CARDS[0].image`, so the visual default is unchanged). - **Phosphor `ArrowRightIcon`.** Replaces the text `→` glyph on the "View other treatment options" link. Direct CSR import (`@phosphor-icons/react/dist/csr/ArrowRight`) matches the convention used elsewhere in the codebase. - **Horizontally scrollable thumb strip with scroll affordance.** - Container is now `overflow-x-auto snap-x snap-mandatory` with the repo's standard scrollbar-hide utilities (`[scrollbar-width:none] [&::-webkit-scrollbar]:hidden`), matching `Doctors.tsx` / `Reviews.tsx`. - Each thumb is `shrink-0 snap-start`. - **Edge-fade indicator** via `mask-image` gradient that updates from a `scroll` listener + `ResizeObserver`: right-only fade at the start, both edges mid-scroll, left-only at the end, no mask when everything fits. Mask (not colored gradient) so it works over the dynamic `config.heroGradient` without needing to match a background color. ### 3. Gallery image expansion — `96563de2b` - `packages/ed-content/src/ed-products.ts`: 4 shared lifestyle URLs appended to every product gallery and to every `galleryByDosage` variant (Sildenafil 50/100mg, Viagra 25/50/100mg, Tadalafil, Cialis). - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/products/ed/[slug]/ProductDetailsClient.tsx`: swap the "Ongoing support" feature-card image to the unified asset; minor formatting cleanup. ### 4. Out-of-stock handling — `ba7fd42ee` - `packages/ed-content/src/ed-products.ts`: `outOfStock: true` on the Cialis entry. - `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/NewGorockyOrderPage.tsx`: the inline brand-card map now reads `outOfStock` and, when true, renders the Cialis card as a `
` (not a ``), with a grayscale-muted product image, an "Out of stock" chip alongside the existing brand chip, a muted price color, and a non-actionable "Currently unavailable" label replacing the View Treatment button. `aria-label` communicates the state to assistive tech. - `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/[slug]/ProductDetailsClient.tsx`: - Reads `product.outOfStock` via `getEdProduct(config.slug)`. - Threads `isOutOfStock` into `SelectorPanel`. - Inline status banner under the description: *"Currently out of stock — we'll restock soon. Talk to our doctor for alternatives in the meantime."* - Primary CTA is replaced with a disabled "Out of stock" `Button` when OOS. - `StickyCheckoutBar` is not rendered when OOS. - "Talk to our Doctor" link below remains as the secondary path. **Legacy `/order/gorocky` already filters OOS products out entirely** (pre-existing behavior, unchanged) — only the new design needed the in-place "show with OOS badge" treatment. ## Test plan ### Order header - [ ] Visit `/order/gorocky` — header shows centered GoRocky logo, no nav links, no hamburger, does not stick on scroll. - [ ] Visit `/order/gorocky/sildenafil` (or any in-stock slug) — same `OrderHeader`. - [ ] Visit any non-order page (`/treatments/gorocky`, `/blog`, `/`) — the full site `Header` still ships there. ### ED PDP polish - [ ] On a product detail page, scroll to "A complete care experience" — image visibly taller on mobile and desktop than before. - [ ] Hover the first feature card → main image swaps. Move mouse to the second → swaps. Move mouse *off* both cards → the last hovered image persists (does not revert). - [ ] Click a card → image stays on that card. - [ ] On `/order/gorocky/sildenafil`, the "View other treatment options" link shows a Phosphor right-arrow icon aligned with the text baseline. - [ ] Thumb strip under the hero image: more thumbs than fit → can scroll horizontally. Scrolling reveals/hides edge fade-masks depending on position. Each thumb snaps when scrolling. - [ ] Change dosage on a multi-dosage product (e.g. Sildenafil 50mg → 100mg) — gallery resets to thumb 0 and fade indicators re-evaluate (no stuck fade when there are fewer thumbs). ### Gallery assets - [ ] On Sildenafil, Viagra, Tadalafil, and Cialis (the OOS PDP still renders) — gallery strip shows the four new lifestyle images at the end. - [ ] On apps/web ED detail mirror — "Ongoing support" feature card renders the unified image. ### Out-of-stock (Cialis) - [ ] `/order/gorocky` — Cialis brand card renders with grayscale image, "Out of stock" chip next to "by Eli Lilly", muted price, "Currently unavailable" label (not a clickable button). Hover does *not* show shadow. Clicking the card body does *not* navigate. - [ ] Tab to the Cialis card — focus order skips it (no ``); screen reader announces the `aria-label`. - [ ] `/order/gorocky/cialis` — page still loads (good for SEO / inbound links). OOS banner visible under the description. Primary CTA is disabled and reads "Out of stock". Sticky checkout bar does not appear when scrolling past the hero. "Talk to our Doctor" still works. - [ ] Flip `outOfStock` back to `false` on Cialis locally — the catalog card and PDP return to fully-functional state with no leftover OOS UI. ## Notes / follow-ups - The `outOfStock` field already existed on `EdProduct` and was honored by `LegacyGorockyOrderPage`, but the new design's brand card and detail page had no awareness of it. This PR closes the gap; adding a future OOS product is now a one-line data change. - 6 commits, isolated by theme — easy to revert any single piece if needed. --- Need help on this PR? Tag @codesmith with what you need. - [ ] Let Codesmith autofix CI failures and bot reviews

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/duplicate treatments pages webapp
GoRocky/core.description: # Duplicate Treatment Pages to Web App ## Summary Copies the GoSlim (`/treatments/goslim`) and GoFuller (`/treatments/gofuller`) marketing pages from `apps/webflow` into `apps/web` so the patient-facing app serves these pages directly — no more redirecting users to `gorocky.ph`. - **8 GoSlim components**: hero, UVP bar, packages, care team, process steps, weight calculator, compounded meds explainer, testimonials with before/after photos, doctor feature - **5 GoFuller components**: hero, UVP marquee, packages, process steps, reviews carousel with auto-play - **Shared components**: `ProcessSection` (reusable step timeline), `ReviewsioLogo` SVG - **Homepage links updated**: "Lose Weight" and "Regrow Hair" category cards now point to the internal routes instead of external `gorocky.ph` URLs ## Key changes from webflow originals | What | Webflow | Web | |------|---------|-----| | Header / Footer | Included per page | Provided by `(public)` layout — removed from pages | | CTA links | `https://forms.gorocky.ph/forms/...` | `/forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2`, `/forms/gofuller-assessment`, `/forms/gorocky-wl-trial-assessment` | | Color tokens (HL/WL) | Defined in webflow globals.css | Already existed in web globals.css from main | | Marquee animation | `animate-marquee-uvp` in webflow CSS | Added to web globals.css | | `FeaturedOnSection` | Accepts `className` prop | Added `className` prop to existing component | ## Files added (21 files, +2,760 lines) ``` apps/web/src/app/(public)/treatments/gofuller/page.tsx # GoFuller page apps/web/src/app/(public)/treatments/goslim/page.tsx # GoSlim page (replaces old consult page) apps/web/src/app/(public)/treatments/goslim-legacy-consult/ # Old goslim consult page preserved here apps/web/src/components/treatments/gofuller/ # 5 GoFuller section components apps/web/src/components/treatments/goslim/ # 8 GoSlim section components apps/web/src/components/treatments/shared/ProcessSection.tsx # Reusable step timeline apps/web/src/components/ui/ReviewsioLogo.tsx # Reviews.io SVG logo ``` ## Files modified - `apps/web/src/app/globals.css` — added `animate-marquee-uvp` keyframes - `apps/web/src/components/landing-page/FeaturedOnSection.tsx` — added `className` prop - `apps/web/src/components/landing-page/HomeCategoriesSection.tsx` — updated category card hrefs to internal routes ## Test plan - [ ] Visit `/treatments/goslim` — verify all sections render (hero, packages, calculator, testimonials, etc.) - [ ] Visit `/treatments/gofuller` — verify all sections render (hero, UVP marquee, packages, process, reviews carousel) - [ ] Click CTA buttons on both pages — should navigate to internal form routes (`/forms/...`) - [ ] Homepage category cards ("Lose Weight", "Regrow Hair") should link to `/treatments/goslim` and `/treatments/gofuller` - [ ] `/treatments/goslim-legacy-consult` — old consultation page is still accessible - [ ] Mobile responsive — check both pages on small screens - [ ] GoFuller reviews carousel auto-plays and pauses on hover - [ ] GoSlim weight calculator slider works --- Need help on this PR? Tag @codesmith with what you need. - [ ] Let Codesmith autofix CI failures and bot reviews

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/subscriptions audit
GoRocky/core.description: # feature/subscriptions-audit — PR summary A grab-bag of fixes and polish across the subscription, forms, scheduling, and design-system surfaces. 27 commits ahead of `main`. Changes are grouped below by area, each with the URL where the work is visible and a placeholder for a screenshot. > Web app runs on `localhost:3001`. Admin app on `localhost:3002`. Dev autofill is available on every form via the **Dev AutoFill** toolbar in the bottom-left (development only). --- ## 1. Subscription order display **What changed** - Subscription anchor orders no longer render as "Product / 3-Month Subscription" on the dashboard. The transform now picks the real underlying product line and prepends `{N}-Month Plan` to the subtitle. - Dashboard orders list → http://localhost:3001/dashboard/orders - Mixed-cart subscriptions (subscription + one-time addon) used to write `JSON.stringify({})` for the addon line, which surfaced as "Unknown Product". The subscription saga now resolves the vari ant and writes a real `productName` / `variantName` / `images` snapshot. - Older order snapshots with `null` or stale relative-path images (`/images/Finoxidil Capsule.webp`) now hydrate from live `products.images` via a JOIN in the orders repository — frozen-in-time snapshots with valid absolute URLs are left alone. --- ## 2. Form runtime — DOB validation + lone asterisk **What changed** - Inline date-of-birth validation now fires across `QuestionRenderer` and `FieldRendererV2` via a shared `validateDateOfBirthValue` helper (parses, not in the future, ≥18, ≤120). Belt-and-suspenders re-check at section-validate time so a stale Zod schema can't accept a bad birthday. - Forms where the section title carries the question (so `question.prompt` is empty) no longer render a lone red `*` on its own line. - Lone-asterisk fix — http://localhost:3001/forms/gofuller-assessment → advance to **Which statement sounds most like you?** (treatment-preference); section title carries the question, so the asterisk row should now be absent --- ## 3. Form fixtures (dev autofill) — gofuller + WL **What changed** - `gofuller-assessment` fixture rebuilt to match the live template after migrations 262 → 279 → 310 → 318 → 319. Drops `hl_consent` / `marketing_consent` (HLWelcomePage's checkboxes are local state, never persisted), replaces `first_name + last_name` with `legal_full_name`, fixes `age` (now a date), and adds the missing `gender` and `treatment_preference` keys. New `hl-female` scenario exercises the family-recommendation gender branch. - `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2` fixture rebuilt against the same template tree. `WL_BASE` now covers every required question across all 13 sections (consent → personal-info → goals → measurements → demographics → medical history → family medical history → current medications → medical background → lifestyle → glp1-experience → treatment preferences). Fixes `treatment_preference` → `treatment_affordability` (correct key + valid value `"balanced"`). Adds missing `has_prescription`. Drops the now-impossible `wl-no-email` scenario (migration 315 made email required). Doctor-clearance and prescription-denial scenarios now also fill `medical_interventions` (revealed when `medical_conditions ≠ ["none"]`). **Where to capture** - Open Dev AutoFill toolbar on http://localhost:3001/forms/gofuller-assessment → run each scenario (`hl-standard`, `hl-severe-loss`, `hl-female`) → confirm every section auto-fills end-to-end - Same on http://localhost:3001/forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2 → run `wl-standard`, then `wl-female`, then each terminal-screen scenario (`wl-doctor-clearance-pancreatitis`, `wl-prescription-denial-mtc`, etc.) --- ## 4. Personalized-plan flow **What changed** - WL page (`/personalized-plan/wl`) now wraps its section switcher in `AnimatePresence` + a keyed `motion.div` using `fadeInUp` and `springs.smooth` from `@gorocky/goforms-ds`. Mirrors the existing HL page transition. Scroll resets to top on each section change. - ConsultDoctorModal (the app version, used by 8 surfaces) now fades the backdrop, scale-fades the panel via `springs.smooth`, and stagger-reveals its inner sections (fee badge, steps, CTA). Respects `useReducedMotion`. The DS-package version of the same modal already had this; the two now feel identical. - HL `EligibilityCard` had `vertical="hm"` (no such vertical) — fixed to `"hg"` (hair growth). - HL post-submit redirect was gated on `categorySlug === 'gofuller'`, but the slug extractor maps gofuller-* forms to `'hl'`. Without the short-circuit, the navigation deferred to the backend and silently routed to legacy checkout when the subscriptions flag was off. Now keyed on `'hl'`. **Where to capture** - WL section transitions — http://localhost:3001/personalized-plan/wl (needs a fresh `submissionToken` in session storage; complete the WL form first, or paste a valid token then refresh) - HL section transitions — http://localhost:3001/personalized-plan/hl (same prerequisite, complete `gofuller-assessment`) - Consult modal — open `/personalized-plan/wl` or `/personalized-plan/hl` and click **Still Unsure? Consult with Our Doc tor** in the plan-selection footer --- ## 5. /schedule + SlotSelector — DS migration **What changed** - Migrated `/schedule` page and `SlotSelector` from hardcoded GoSlim yellow (`#F4D03F` / `#FFFBEA` / `#D4AC0D`) to DS tokens (`--ds-orange-*`, `--ds-text-*`, `--ds-radius-*`). This surface is shared between WL and HL flows, so the GoSlim-specific palette was wrong. - Shadcn CTA → DS `Button variant="primary"` on the missing-info card. DS `Skeleton` ×3 replaces the hand-rolled animated divs in the loading state. - Heading copy is now category-aware: WL eyebrow says "Weight management", HL says "Hair loss". Heading uses the same `#762B00 → #DE7233` gradient as `/dashboard/consultations`. - `LoadingState` extracted into its own component so `page.tsx` and `SchedulePageContent` no longer duplicate the spinner block. - Cal.com `linkFailed` events now render an in-card error message instead of leaving the calendar pane empty (with the toast still firing). This is what showed up when `cal.com/gorocky-medical-team/wl-consults` 404'd. **Where to capture** - WL schedule — http://localhost:3001/schedule?category=wl - HL schedule — http://localhost:3001/schedule?category=hl - Loading skeleton — same URL with throttled network - Cal.com error fallback — same URL (the live link 404s, so the in-card error renders by default until the slug is fixed) --- ## 7. GoSlim polish + design system showcase **What changed** - `GoSlimPersonalDetailsPage` focus rings: the `focus:ring-(--form-primary)` classes already existed but `--form-primary` was never defined in the app, so the browser fell back to its default brown outline. Now bound from `theme.primaryHex` via `FormThemeProvider` on the form wrapper, so focus rings match the theme. - `GoSlimCannotPrescribe` and `GoSlimClearToMeetDoctor` migrated to DS tokens + DS `Button` (separate commits earlier in the branch). - Consultations dashboard heading uses the DS gradient style; testimonial card surface swapped from cold gray to warm `#fefaf5`. - New `/dev/design-system` is the showcase v2 (ported from `apps/goforms`, lives under the `goforms-ds` package). Old shadcn-based showcase moved to `/dev/design-system/legacy`. **Where to capture** - WL personal-info focus rings — http://localhost:3001/forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2 → advance to **Personal Information** → focus an input - Consultations dashboard — http://localhost:3001/dashboard/consultations - Design system showcase v2 — http://localhost:3001/dev/design-system - Legacy showcase (for comparison) — http://localhost:3001/dev/design-system/legacy - --- ## 8. Subscription offers admin tab **What changed** - New per-product **Subscription Offers** tab under product detail in the admin app, for managing offers tied to a product. **Where to capture** - Admin product detail with Subscription Offers tab — http://localhost:3002/products/{productId} → open the **Subscription Offers** tab (use a hair or weight-loss product that already has a subscription_product wired up) --- ## 9. Smaller polish (no dedicated screenshots needed) - Checkout — hide "Add New Address" button while the new-address form is open - Checkout — auto-create a checkout session when the flow navigates to the checkout step - Personalized plan — testimonial before/after images moved to CDN; HL brand colors + page-level animations + keyboard navigation; `Enter` key shortcut on HL welcome page - Forms — reduce excessive top padding above mobile content in `FormLayoutWithImage` - Goforms-ds — extract `EligibilityCard` component from `HLYourAssessment` - Contact Us / Identifiers — section styling + Prettier passes - Various DS-token / Prettier formatting passes on `/checkout/success` and `/dev/design-system` --- ## How to set up locally ```bash pnpm install pnpm dev # starts web (3001) + admin (3002) + backend (3000) ``` Useful URLs: | Surface | URL | | --- | --- | | Design system showcase v2 | http://localhost:3001/dev/design-system | | WL form | http://localhost:3001/forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2 | | HL form | http://localhost:3001/forms/gofuller-assessment | | WL personalized plan | http://localhost:3001/personalized-plan/wl | | HL personalized plan | http://localhost:3001/personalized-plan/hl | | Schedule (WL) | http://localhost:3001/schedule?category=wl | | Schedule (HL) | http://localhost:3001/schedule?category=hl | | Cart | http://localhost:3001/cart | | Dashboard home | http://localhost:3001/dashboard/home | | Dashboard orders | http://localhost:3001/dashboard/orders | | Dashboard consultations | http://localhost:3001/dashboard/consultations | | Admin product detail | http://localhost:3002/products/{productId} | To pre-fill a form quickly, click the **Dev AutoFill** button bottom-left and pick a scenario. For pages that need a `submissionToken` in session storage (`/personalized-plan/*`), complete the corresponding form first — the runtime writes the token automatically.

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/design system
GoRocky/core.description:
# Centralized design system + ED redesign behind a permanently-off flag

## Why this PR exists

The primary goal of merging this is to land the **shared design system infrastructure** so other features can build on it. The new ED catalog/detail designs that come along for the ride are **intentionally inaccessible in production** — they're gated behind `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_NEW_ED_DESIGN`, and that env var will not be set in any production build.

In short:
- ✅ **Will affect production**: shared `@gorocky/goforms-ds` + `@gorocky/ed-content` packages, animation primitives, ED design tokens, code reorganization
- ❌ **Will not affect production**: the new `/products/ed` and `/order/gorocky` redesigns — gated off, prod still renders the legacy layouts

This is deliberate. The redesign work was scaffolded, polished, and committed so the shared components had a real consumer driving them, but the actual design rollout decision is paused.

---

## What lands in `main` and is live

### 1. `@gorocky/goforms-ds` — shared design-system package
A workspace package other apps can consume. Currently exports:

| Export | Type |
|---|---|
| `Button`, `SelectionCard`, `BooleanCards`, `TextInput`, `TextArea`, `DateInput`, `FileUpload`, `ConsentCheckbox` | Form primitives |
| `ProgressBar`, `FormNavbar`, `StickyFooter`, `ReassuranceStrip`, `WhyWeAsk`, `MomentumMessage` | Form layout |
| `SectionTransition`, `Skeleton`, `InfoDisplay`, `GrainOverlay` | Visual primitives |
| `ConfirmationModal`, `ConsultDoctorModal` | Modals (with motion / backdrop animations) |
| `RevealGroup`, `RevealItem`, `RevealSection` | Scroll-triggered reveal primitives |
| `springs`, `colors`, `fadeInUp`, `staggerContainer`, `staggerItem` | Motion + color tokens |
| `timing`, `verticalConfig`, `getVerticalFromCategory` | Behavioral timing constants |
| Stylesheet at `@gorocky/goforms-ds/styles` | `.ds-grain`, `.ds-skeleton`, `.ds-spinner`, ds-* CSS variables |

**Why it matters:** future feature work (admin redesigns, new flows, etc.) can import from one place instead of duplicating motion/modal/animation code per app. Both `apps/web` and `apps/webflow` are now wired to consume it.

### 2. `@gorocky/ed-content` — shared ED catalog content
Extracts ED product data and design tokens out of `apps/webflow/src/data/` into a workspace package both apps can read.

- `src/ed-products.ts` — single source of truth for product slugs, variants, prices, checkout URLs, galleries, per-dosage galleries, legacy packshots
- `src/ed-product-details.ts` — per-product detail page configs (copy, dosages, FAQs, related products)
- `ed-tokens.css` — ED-specific colors + gradients (`--gradient-ed-*`, `--color-ed-*`)

**Why it matters:** edits to variant prices, checkout URLs, packshots, etc. now happen in one place and propagate to every consumer.

### 3. Component sharing across `web` and `webflow`
- `<ConsultDoctorModal>` previously duplicated in two apps → now lives in `@gorocky/goforms-ds` once
- `<RevealGroup>` / `<RevealItem>` / `<RevealSection>` previously duplicated in two apps → now in DS once
- Pattern: any future shared UI primitive goes through DS, not copy-paste

### 4. Webflow `StickyCheckoutBar` (already on main, refined here)
Migrated to DS tokens, scroll-to-selector "Change" button, inline price/descriptor layout, subscription billing support.

### 5. Code-quality fixes from review feedback
- Cleared `hoveredFeature` on `onMouseLeave` (real bug)
- Collapsed dead ternaries
- Removed redundant `as true` / `as false` casts
- Simplified subscription suffix check in `StickyCheckoutBar`

---

## What ships but stays dark

### The new ED redesigns
A complete redesign of:
- `/products/ed` (web) and `/order/gorocky` (webflow) — new catalog with hero, main product cards, bundle bar, premium brands, recommendation CTA, trust bar, scroll-reveal animations
- `/products/ed/[slug]` (web) and `/order/gorocky/[slug]` (webflow) — new detail page with gallery, dosage selectors, quote bar, feature cards, how-it-works, benefits grid, competitor comparison, social proof, FAQ, sticky checkout bar, ConsultDoctorModal

These are functional, type-safe, and (when locally enabled) work end-to-end against the legacy Shopify checkout URLs. **They simply don't render in production because the flag is never set there.**

### Routing behavior in prod (flag unset)
| Route | App | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `/products/ed` | web | Renders legacy `<ProductCard>` grid (unchanged from current prod) |
| `/products/ed/[slug]` | web | 307-redirects to `/products/[slug]` (unchanged from current prod) |
| `/order/gorocky` | webflow | Renders legacy `<WebflowProductCard>` grid (matches current prod design) |
| `/order/gorocky/[slug]` | webflow | 404 (legacy never had detail pages) |

Net effect on production: **zero visible change** for end users.

### Why ship gated dead code?
- Provides real-world consumers for the shared DS components, ensuring they aren't theoretical
- Lets future engineers see how the DS pieces compose into a real page
- Allows a future "yes, launch ED redesign" decision to be a one-line env-var change rather than a fresh PR

---

## Test plan

Production-relevant verification (since the redesign is gated off):

- [ ] After deploy, `/products/ed` renders the legacy ProductCard grid identical to today
- [ ] `/products/ed/sildenafil` continues to redirect to `/products/sildenafil`
- [ ] `/order/gorocky` renders the legacy WebflowProductCard grid (same visual as current gorocky.ph)
- [ ] No console errors on either app
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/web exec tsc --noEmit -p .` passes
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @gorocky/webflow exec tsc --noEmit -p .` passes
- [ ] Other features that consume `@gorocky/goforms-ds` still work (forms, modals, animations elsewhere)

---

## Risk assessment

**Low risk.** Production routes are guaranteed to render their legacy implementations because the flag is build-time inlined and explicitly will not be set. Worst case: a small visual diff on legacy `/order/gorocky` due to the DS token import — but the legacy layout itself is recovered verbatim from git history (commit `f7c8715a6`-era), so it should match what's live today.

**Rollback plan:** revert the merge. The shared packages are additive — removing them affects only their (currently new) consumers in this PR.
GoRocky/core.summary: The primary goal of merging this is to land the **shared design system infrastructure** so other features can build on it. The new ED catalog/detail designs that come along for the ride are **intentionally inaccessible in production** — they're gated behind `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_NEW_ED_DESIGN`, and th

GoRocky/core.title: Fix/address clarity recordings struggles
GoRocky/core.description: # Webflow 404 page + footer/redirect cleanup ## Summary This PR ships a custom **404 page** for the GoRocky marketing site (`apps/webflow`), refactors the homepage treatment cards into a shared component, fixes mobile-nav UX in the webflow header, and cleans up two stray link issues in the patient web app and webflow redirects. The scope expanded beyond the original branch name as small adjacent issues surfaced during the 404 work — they're each one- or two-line changes and are bundled here rather than fragmented across separate PRs. ## Changes ### 1. Custom 404 page (`apps/webflow`) A new `not-found.tsx` for the marketing site, rendered automatically by Next.js for any unmatched route. **File**: `apps/webflow/src/app/not-found.tsx` (new) The page is designed to recover lost users by routing them either home or directly into a treatment vertical. Decisions made during shaping: - **Tone**: warm and reassuring with a wink — fits the men's-health audience without being flippant. - **Visual direction**: cream/warm-neutral background (`#fefaf5` → `#faf8f6` → `#fbf9f6`) rather than a loud orange hero band — the page should feel like the brand turning the volume down, not up. - **Headline**: *"Hmm, that page isn't here."* (a soft acknowledgement; the human language carries the meaning while the large `404` mark provides the visual anchor). - **Primary action**: dual-path recovery — a "Back to home" CTA and a 3-card grid of treatment categories (Have Better Sex / Lose Weight / Regrow Hair) reusing the homepage card pattern. - **Help center**: surfaced as a separate full-width card below the treatment grid for users whose problem is "I can't find what I need" rather than "wrong URL." Opens in a new tab and is marked as an external link. - **Accessibility**: focus-visible rings on every interactive element, `aria-label="Error 404"` on the numeric mark for screen-reader context, AA-compliant text contrast, `prefers-reduced-motion` respected on hover transforms. - **Responsive**: type scale ascends `120px → 160px → 200px` from mobile to desktop. All touch targets ≥44px. - **SEO**: `robots: { index: false, follow: false }` — 404s shouldn't be indexed. ### 2. Shared `CategoryCard` component The treatment-card markup (white card with orange-gradient image strip, split heading like *"Have / Better Sex"*) lived inline inside `Categories.tsx` and was needed in two places now (homepage + 404). Extracted into a single source of truth. **Files**: - `apps/webflow/src/components/sections/CategoryCard.tsx` (new) — exports `CategoryCard` component and `treatmentCategories` data - `apps/webflow/src/components/sections/Categories.tsx` (refactored) — now thin wrapper that maps `treatmentCategories` → `CategoryCard`. **Visual output is unchanged.** The shared component also picks up the focus-ring and reduced-motion improvements made for the 404, so the homepage benefits too. ### 3. Mobile menu — close on outside click (`Header.tsx`) Previously, the mobile hamburger menu only closed via the close button or by tapping a link. Tapping anywhere else outside the header left the menu open, which felt broken. Now the mobile menu closes when the user taps outside the header element. **File**: `apps/webflow/src/components/layout/Header.tsx` Implementation extends the existing `mousedown` listener (already used for the desktop dropdowns) with a check against a new `headerRef`. When a click target is outside the header, `closeMobileMenu()` is called, which collapses the menu and any open mobile sub-dropdowns. The hamburger button itself is inside the header, so opening still works normally. ### 4. `/lp/val/eng` 301 redirect (`next.config.ts`) Adds a permanent redirect from the legacy `/lp/val/eng` landing-page URL to the canonical `/order/gorocky` flow. `permanent: true` means a 301 — search engines will update their indexes. **File**: `apps/webflow/next.config.ts` ### 5. Footer help-center link (`apps/web`) The patient web app's footer linked "Help Center" to `https://www.gorocky.ph/help-center`, which 404s. Updated to point to `https://help.gorocky.ph/` (the actual help-center subdomain, also used in the webflow header and the new 404 page's help-center CTA). **File**: `apps/web/src/components/layout/Footer.tsx` ## Test plan - [ ] Visit any unknown URL on the webflow site (e.g. `/does-not-exist`) and confirm the new 404 renders with `Header` + `Footer` chrome - [ ] Confirm response status is `404` (DevTools → Network) - [ ] On desktop: hover each treatment card → verify lift + shadow; hover help-center card → verify border colour shift + arrow translation - [ ] On mobile (<640px): confirm 404 mark scales down, treatment cards stack, "Back to home" button is full-width and tappable - [ ] Tab through the page — every interactive element shows a visible focus ring (orange against white, white against orange) - [ ] Screen reader (VoiceOver / NVDA): the `404` is announced as "Error 404", the help-center link announces "opens in a new tab" - [ ] Trigger `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (DevTools → Rendering) — hover transforms should not animate - [ ] Verify `` is present in page source - [ ] On the homepage, confirm the treatment cards still look identical to before the `CategoryCard` extraction - [ ] On mobile, open the hamburger menu, then tap outside the header — menu should close - [ ] Visit `/lp/val/eng` → confirm 301 redirect to `/order/gorocky` - [ ] On `apps/web`, click the footer "Help Center" link → confirm it opens `help.gorocky.ph` (not the broken `/help-center` path) ## Files changed | File | Change | |---|---| | `apps/webflow/src/app/not-found.tsx` | New — custom 404 page | | `apps/webflow/src/components/sections/CategoryCard.tsx` | New — extracted shared treatment card | | `apps/webflow/src/components/sections/Categories.tsx` | Refactor — use shared `CategoryCard` | | `apps/webflow/src/components/layout/Header.tsx` | Fix — mobile menu closes on outside click | | `apps/webflow/next.config.ts` | Add — 301 redirect for `/lp/val/eng` | | `apps/web/src/components/layout/Footer.tsx` | Fix — help-center URL | ## Notes - **No new dependencies.** - **No design tokens were used** — `apps/webflow` doesn't currently have a token system; brand hexes (`#d64f00`, `#481800`, `#fefaf5`, etc.) are hard-coded across the marketing site. The 404 follows the same convention. A project-level tokenization pass would be a separate effort. - **The `Back to home` button reuses the existing webflow CTA dimensions** found in `ProductInfoPage.tsx` (`px-8 py-3.5 font-bold tracking-wide transition inline-block`) so it feels native to the site, with a slightly softer `rounded-xl` and `text-md` chosen for this surface. --- Need help on this PR? Tag @codesmith with what you need. - [ ] Let Codesmith autofix CI failures and bot reviews

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/meta page alignment
GoRocky/core.description: # Align meta treatment pages with main treatment pages Branch: `feature/meta-page-alignment` → `main` ## Summary Brings the three `/meta/treatments/*` landing pages (GoRocky, GoSlim, GoFuller) into structural parity with their canonical `/treatments/*` counterparts by replacing duplicated `meta-*` components with the shared treatment components and the reusable `ProcessSection`. ## Changes by page ### `apps/webflow/src/app/meta/treatments/gofuller/page.tsx` - Removed ~140 lines of inline hero markup (icons, bullet points, `MetaGofullerHero`) previously living in the page file. - Swapped `MetaGofullerPackages` and `MetaGofullerEducational` for the shared `GofullerHero`, `GofullerUvp`, `GofullerPackages`, and `GofullerReviews` components. - Added `FeaturedOn` section. ### `apps/webflow/src/app/meta/treatments/gorocky/page.tsx` - Replaced `MetaGorockyEducational` with `GorockyDoctorFeature` + `FeaturedOn`. - Now uses the same `ProcessSection` configuration as the main GoRocky page (1:1 parity). ### `apps/webflow/src/app/meta/treatments/goslim/page.tsx` - Replaced page-specific `GoslimProcess` with the shared `ProcessSection` (imported from gorocky's location) plus `GoslimCareTeam`. - Process step copy, CTA, image, and color tokens now match the main GoSlim experience. ## Diff stat ``` apps/webflow/src/app/meta/treatments/gofuller/page.tsx | 158 ++------------- apps/webflow/src/app/meta/treatments/gorocky/page.tsx | 59 +++--- apps/webflow/src/app/meta/treatments/goslim/page.tsx | 45 ++++- 3 files changed, 94 insertions(+), 168 deletions(-) ``` Net: **−74 lines**, three pages converging on shared components. ## Commits - `958ae6047` feat(meta/gorocky): make treatments and meta treatments for gorocky 1-1 - `0a56c61f6` refactor(meta/gofuller): remove MetaGofullerEducational section and clean up imports - `d8a467b58` refactor(meta/gofuller): replace Meta components with GoFuller-specific components and update page layout - `10cecadfd` refactor(meta/goslim): replace GoslimProcess with GoslimCareTeam and shared ProcessSection ## Notes / follow-ups - `ProcessSection` is currently imported from `@/components/treatments/gorocky/GorockyProcess` even when used by GoSlim. Worth lifting to a brand-neutral path (e.g. `@/components/sections/ProcessSection`) in a follow-up — the component already takes all brand colors as props. - `MetaGorockyEducational` import is removed but the JSX is left commented out in `gorocky/page.tsx`. Either restore or delete before merge. - `MetaGofullerPackages` / `MetaGofullerEducational` / `MetaGorockyEducational` components are no longer referenced by these pages — confirm no other callers, then delete in a cleanup PR. ## Test plan - [ ] Visit `/meta/treatments/gorocky` and confirm visual parity with `/treatments/gorocky` - [ ] Visit `/meta/treatments/goslim` and confirm process steps, CTA, and color tokens render correctly - [ ] Visit `/meta/treatments/gofuller` and confirm hero, UVP, packages, reviews, FeaturedOn render in order - [ ] Verify all CTAs route to the correct assessment forms - [ ] Mobile + desktop spot-check --- Need help on this PR? Tag @codesmith with what you need. - [ ] Let Codesmith autofix CI failures and bot reviews

GoRocky/core.title: Fix/webflow UI fixes
GoRocky/core.description: # Webflow UI polish: carousels, GoSlim hero/testimonials, Montserrat cleanup ## Summary Tightens a few pieces of the `apps/webflow` migration: 1. **Mobile-swipeable carousels** — Doctors (homepage) and GoFuller Reviews now support native touch swipe with reliable dot-pagination sync. Auto-play pauses while the user is swiping and resumes after. 2. **GoSlim landing polish** — hero and testimonials sections re-tuned for readability on mobile and for better proportion on larger breakpoints. 3. **Typography cleanup** — all `font-montserrat` class usages removed across the webflow app (32 files). Components now inherit the app-wide default (Radio Canada). The Montserrat font itself is left loaded; full removal of the import/utility is noted as pending in `CONVERSION_STATUS.md`. Scope is entirely `apps/webflow`. No DB, backend, API, or goforms/web app changes. ## Commits - `3fcb5bc8f` — feat(gofuller): improve reviews carousel scroll sync and mobile touch handling - `bb0397ceb` — feat: remove all instances of font-montserrat - `789561885` — feat(webflow): improve doctors carousel scroll sync and mobile touch handling - `aa3d68626` — feat(goslim): adjust hero section layout and typography for larger breakpoints - `4f6539ad0` — feat(goslim): refine testimonials section layout and styling for mobile and desktop ## Details ### 1. Carousel: mobile swipe + pagination sync Applied the same three fixes to both `Doctors.tsx` and `GofullerReviews.tsx`: - **`overflow-hidden` → `overflow-x-auto`** with `overscroll-x-contain` and `-webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch` — unlocks native horizontal touch swipe while preventing the swipe from bubbling to the page or triggering back-navigation gestures on iOS. - **`onTouchStart={pauseAutoPlay}` + `onTouchEnd={resumeAutoPlay}`** — mirrors hover behavior for touch so auto-play doesn't fight a user mid-swipe. - **Added `scrollend` listener** alongside the existing `scroll` listener, with an equality-guarded `setCurrentPage` — dots update reliably after a touch swipe settles on the snap point (iOS 16.4+ / Chrome 114+); the `scroll` listener keeps working as a fallback on older browsers. ### 2. GoSlim hero + testimonials - `GoslimHero.tsx` — typography scale tuned for `lg`/`xl` breakpoints so the hero doesn't visually collapse on wide monitors. - `GoslimTestimonials.tsx` — mobile spacing and card layout tightened; desktop alignment cleaned. ### 3. Montserrat class removal (32 files) - All `font-montserrat` class tokens stripped from `className="..."`, `className={\`...\`}`, and string args inside `cn()`/`cx()`/`clsx()` calls across 32 component/page files. - Double-space artifacts inside class strings collapsed; no non-class string literals were touched. - `layout.tsx`, `tailwind.config.ts`, and `globals.css` are unchanged — the font is still loaded and the utility still exists for the inline `style={{ fontFamily: "var(--font-montserrat)" }}` usages in `product-info/*` (left alone intentionally). - `CONVERSION_STATUS.md` now has a "Typography cleanup" section documenting what was removed and what's still pending. ## Risk / blast radius - **Webflow app only.** No runtime logic, no data, no shared packages. - **Visual regression risk is highest on Montserrat cleanup** (32 files). Components now render in Radio Canada. For most sections this is aligned with the rest of the app; please eyeball the screenshots before merging. ## Test plan - [x] Visit the home page on a real mobile device or narrow browser — swipe the Doctors carousel left/right; dots should match the visible page. - [x] Same test on `/treatments/gofuller` → "Rooted in Results" section. - [x] Confirm auto-play still advances every few seconds when idle, and pauses during a swipe. - [x] Scroll `/` at `lg`+ and confirm the GoSlim hero looks right. - [x] Spot-check any 3–4 pages in the Montserrat screenshot list below for obvious font regressions (no missed spacing, no broken line-heights). - [x] Lighthouse / network tab: confirm no new font files are being loaded (Montserrat loading unchanged). ### Carousel changes — record short screencasts (GIF or .mov), not stills 1. **`/` — Doctors carousel** on mobile (iPhone-size): swipe left/right, show dots tracking the active card. Before: ![Doctor Carousel scroll (before)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/539a19a2-123d-4ced-9063-114a1f7367d3) After: ![Doctor Carousel scroll (after)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77302f57-c391-4adc-b722-58caf02cc471) 2. **`/treatments/gofuller` → "Rooted in Results" section** on mobile: same — swipe + dot sync. Before: After: ### GoSlim visual tweaks — stills 3. **`/treatments/goslim`** — hero, **desktop `xl`** (≥1440px). 4. **`/treatments/goslim`** — testimonials, **mobile** (~390px). 5. **`/treatments/goslim`** — testimonials, **desktop**. ### Montserrat removal — stills (for visual regression review) Capture at both **mobile (~390px)** and **desktop (≥1280px)** where the page has substantial text. These are the most Montserrat-heavy pages based on the file list: 6. **`/order/goslim/weight-loss-basic`** — whole page (product card, description, CTA). 7. **`/about`** — whole page (hero, who-we-are, milestones, treatments, founders, cta). 8. **`/blog/*`** — pick any published post (exercises `DoctorProfile`, `KeyTakeaways`, `ReferencesAccordion`, `EditorialStandards`). 9. **`/treatments/govax`** — whole page (hero, stats, doses, options, protect, partners). 10. **`/treatments/gorocky`** — hero + doctor feature + packages. 11. **`/meta/treatments/gofuller`** — whole page (educational, packages, the `meta-*` variants). 12. **`/partner/goslim-ekonsulta`** — doctors, why-join, testimonials. 13. **`/`** — home page Doctors section (checks the inline header text font after Montserrat removal). 14. **Any product-info page** using `ProductInfoPage`, `FAQAccordion`, `TestimonialsSlider` — these still use `style={{ fontFamily: "var(--font-montserrat)" }}` inline, so they should be **unchanged**; screenshot as a control. ## Rollback Pure frontend, single-app. Revert the branch or cherry-pick out individual commits. No migrations to roll back.

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/webflow ordering flow UI revamp 2026
GoRocky/core.description: # Webflow Ordering Flow UI Revamp + Web App Port Brings the new ED ordering flow design — first built on `/apps/webflow` at `/order/gorocky` — into `/apps/web` at `/products/ed`. Ships behind a feature flag so production stays on the legacy design until launch. > **🚦 Pre-launch state:** New design is **off** in production by default. Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_NEW_ED_DESIGN=true` in dev/staging to preview. --- ## What's new ### 1. New ED catalog page A redesigned `/products/ed` (and webflow `/order/gorocky`) with hero, main product cards (Sildenafil, Tadalafil), bundle bar, premium brands (Viagra, Cialis), recommendation CTA, and trust bar — all with staggered scroll-reveal animations. > 📸 **Screenshot:** desktop catalog > 📸 **Screenshot:** mobile catalog ### 2. New product detail page A redesigned `/products/ed/[slug]` (and webflow `/order/gorocky/[slug]`) with: - Image gallery with thumbnail switcher (per-dosage galleries on Viagra + Sildenafil) - Dosage + quantity selector with subscription support - Quote bar with trust pills - "More than just medication" feature cards - How-it-works timeline - Benefits grid with hover/pinned interactions - "Why GoRocky" competitor comparison - Social proof + reviews - FAQ accordion - Final CTA > 📸 **Screenshot:** desktop product detail (full page or hero + selector) > 📸 **Screenshot:** mobile product detail ### 3. Sticky checkout bar Slides up from the bottom once the user scrolls past the primary CTA. Shows inline price · descriptor · "Change" button · Checkout. The "Change" button smooth-scrolls back to the variant selector. > 📸 **Screenshot:** sticky checkout bar (desktop + mobile) ### 4. Per-dosage image galleries Viagra (25mg / 50mg / 100mg) and Sildenafil (50mg / 100mg) now show distinct packshots when the user toggles the dosage. Powered by `galleryByDosage` on each product entry. > 📸 **Screenshot / GIF:** dosage toggle swapping the gallery ### 5. Consult Doctor modal "Talk to our Doctor" link opens a contextual modal styled to the active vertical (ed-2026 here). > 📸 **Screenshot:** consult doctor modal --- ## Architecture changes ### `@gorocky/ed-content` shared package — `packages/ed-content/` Extracted ED catalog data + design tokens out of `apps/webflow/src/data/` so both `apps/web` and `apps/webflow` consume the same source of truth. | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `src/ed-products.ts` | Product catalog (slugs, variants, prices, galleries, `galleryByDosage`) | | `src/ed-product-details.ts` | Per-product detail page configs (copy, dosages, quote, FAQs, related products) | | `ed-tokens.css` | ED-specific semantic colors + gradients (`--gradient-ed-*`, `--color-ed-*`) | ### Animation primitives — `apps/{web,webflow}/src/components/animation/Reveal.tsx` - `` — stagger container, triggers on scroll into view - `` — individual fade + slide-up child - `` — single-unit reveal for whole sections - Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` ### Hybrid data model on the catalog `/apps/web/products/ed` blends: - **Live DB pricing** via `useProductsByCategory("ED")` (matched to static slugs through `DB_SLUG_BY_STATIC`) - **Static marketing content** from `@gorocky/ed-content` (gradients, taglines, badges, gallery) Falls back to static config price when the DB product isn't found. ### Preserved `/products/ed` behavior The flag-gated new catalog **still uses**: - `useCategoryFlow("ed")` for gating - `` for authenticated, form-complete users - `analytics.trackEvent("product_list_viewed", …)` - The `EdProductDetailsFrame` wrapper on detail pages handles the same gating + redirects disqualified users to `/disqualified?category=ed` --- ## Feature flag ### `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_NEW_ED_DESIGN` | Value | `/products/ed` (web) | `/products/ed/[slug]` (web) | `/order/gorocky` + `/[slug]` (webflow) | |---|---|---|---| | unset / `false` (prod default) | Renders `` | `redirect('/products/[slug]')` | `notFound()` (404) | | `true` (dev / staging) | New design | New design | New design | - Helper: `apps/{web,webflow}/src/lib/flags/ed-redesign.ts` - Build-time inlined (`NEXT_PUBLIC_*`) — set in your build environment - AWS Secrets Manager **cannot** drive this; it's skipped via `skipNextPublic: true` - Documented in `apps/webflow/.env.example` > 📸 **Screenshot (optional):** legacy `/products/ed` (flag OFF) for visual diff > > _add image here_ --- ## Routes affected | Route | App | When flag is ON | When flag is OFF | |---|---|---|---| | `/products/ed` | web | New catalog | Legacy catalog (unchanged) | | `/products/ed/[slug]` | web | New detail page | 307 → `/products/[slug]` | | `/order/gorocky` | webflow | New catalog | 404 | | `/order/gorocky/[slug]` | webflow | New detail page | 404 | --- ## Files of note - `packages/ed-content/` — new workspace package - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/products/ed/page.tsx` — flag switcher - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/products/ed/{Legacy,New}EdProductsPage.tsx` - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/products/ed/[slug]/page.tsx` — server wrapper with redirect - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/products/ed/[slug]/EdProductDetailsFrame.tsx` — `useCategoryFlow` integration - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/products/ed/[slug]/ProductDetailsClient.tsx` — main detail page - `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/{page,[slug]/page,[slug]/ProductDetailsClient}.tsx` — webflow tree - `apps/webflow/src/components/product/StickyCheckoutBar.tsx` — sticky CTA - `apps/webflow/src/components/order/ConsultDoctorModal.tsx` — modal (also copied into web) - `apps/web/src/lib/flags/ed-redesign.ts` + `apps/webflow/src/lib/flags/ed-redesign.ts` --- ## Test plan - [ ] **Flag OFF (production parity)** - [ ] `/products/ed` renders the existing legacy ProductCard grid - [ ] `/products/ed/sildenafil` 307-redirects to `/products/sildenafil` - [ ] `/order/gorocky` returns 404 - [ ] No console errors on either app - [ ] **Flag ON (new design)** - [ ] `/products/ed` — hero, 2 main cards, bundle bar, brand cards, CTA, trust bar all render - [ ] Scroll-reveal animations fire once each section enters viewport - [ ] Catalog cards link to `/products/ed/[slug]` - [ ] `/products/ed/sildenafil` shows new layout with gallery - [ ] Dosage toggle (Sildenafil 50→100mg) changes price + gallery - [ ] Dosage toggle (Viagra 25→50→100mg) shows three distinct packshot galleries - [ ] Sticky checkout bar appears on scroll past the primary CTA - [ ] Sticky bar "Change" button smooth-scrolls back to selector panel - [ ] "Talk to our Doctor" opens the ConsultDoctorModal - [ ] **Auth-gated behavior preserved** - [ ] Authenticated user (form complete, not disqualified) → `` renders at top - [ ] Authenticated disqualified user lands on detail → redirected to `/disqualified?category=ed` - [ ] Anonymous user can still browse without errors - [ ] **Analytics** - [ ] `product_list_viewed` event fires once per catalog visit - [ ] `product_detail_viewed` event fires on detail page mount - [ ] **Responsive** - [ ] Catalog mobile (≤ 767px): single-column stack, hero text panel, bundle bar wraps below art - [ ] Detail page mobile: gallery thumbnails, selector below hero, sticky bar adapts to viewport - [ ] **`prefers-reduced-motion`** — animations short-circuit to instant fades - [ ] **TypeScript**: `pnpm --filter @gorocky/web exec tsc --noEmit -p .` and the same for `@gorocky/webflow` both pass > 📸 **Screenshot:** any final QA pass (lighthouse / network panel / etc.) > > _add image here_ --- ## Deployment notes 1. Merge to `main` while flag is unset → zero behavior change in prod ✅ 2. To launch: - Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_NEW_ED_DESIGN=true` in the production build environment - Trigger a deploy (build-time variable, requires rebuild — not runtime-toggleable) 3. To roll back: - Unset / set to `false` in the build environment - Redeploy > ⚠️ Reminder: this is a **build-time** flag. Flipping it does not take effect until the next deploy. --- ## Out of scope / follow-ups - Sildenafil 100mg packshots are currently the same images as 50mg. If/when distinct packshots are shot, swap the `"100 mg"` array in `galleryByDosage`. - Sticky checkout bar's `desktopSelectorWrapperRef` / `mobileSelectorWrapperRef` are wired through, but the responsive single-tree refactor uses `desktopSelectorWrapperRef` only (mobile ref is unused — `handleChangeClick` falls back gracefully). - Subscription billing display in the sticky bar is plumbed (`StickyCheckoutBarBilling`) but not yet driven by config. --- Codesmith can help with this PR, just tag @codesmith or enable autofix. Settings. - [ ] Autofix CI and bot reviews

GoRocky/core.title: Fix/goforms UI polish
GoRocky/core.description:
# GoForms UI polish + ED assessment shell fixes

## Summary

A pass of UX / visual polish across the GoForms renderer and the `gorocky-ed-assessment` shell (DSFormShell). Most changes are small but they add up to a much more coherent flow: consistent brand theming, no more orphan asterisks or duplicate question text, proper back navigation, and the new GoRocky navbar wired into all custom components (consent, contact details, nitrates check).

## Changes

### Typography & theming
- Swap primary font from **Geist → Radio Canada**
- Rebrand `goslim-assessment-v2` theme to GoRocky orange
- Replace hardcoded `#d64f00` hover border on radio cards with `var(--form-primary)` so hover colors follow the brand
- Standardize ED design-system hex fallbacks and adjust section header spacing
- Unify Continue-button styling to use the same CSS variables as the core renderer (HLHairLossScale, others)

### Form renderer behavior
- **Disable Continue when required fields are empty** — works across radio, checkbox group, boolean, and text types, respecting conditional visibility
- **Fix back navigation** — removed `skipContentOnlyOnBack` from `handleBack` so users can actually return to the landing / consent / contact pages
- **Hide section description** when it duplicates the field label (moved to field-driven heading pattern)
- **Hide required asterisk** when the question has no label text (prevents orphan `*` on button-group questions)
- Remove unused `ArrowLeft` import and other small cleanups

### DSFormShell (gorocky-ed-assessment shell)
- **Render the shared GoRocky navbar on all custom components** (consent, contact details, nitrates) instead of letting each file render its own — with an explicit `SELF_HEADERED_COMPONENTS` opt-out for landing / confidence-transition / personalized-plan screens
- **Support both `medical-conditions` and `medical_conditions` section slugs** so the "Your data is encrypted" reassurance strip renders on production forms, not just the V2 mock
- Remove duplicate internal `FormNavbar` from `GoRockyConsentPage`, `GoRockyContactDetails`, and `NitratesCheckSection`

### Personalized-plan screens
- Hide the shared navbar for assessment / product-recommendation / plan-selection / loading screens (HL, GoSlim, GoRocky, ED) — they own their own header

### Gofuller-specific
- Drop the GoRocky header from `HLWelcomePage`
- Rework `HLHairLossScale` layout to match the design system

### Custom components
- Implement `GoRockyConfidenceTransition` component with design-system integration
- Remove the internal `FormNavbar` calls from GoRocky custom components now that DSFormShell renders the shared one

### DB migration
- **279_rename_gofuller_age_verification_section.sql** — rename the "Age Verification" section to "Date of Birth" on `gofuller-assessment` and convert the question from a number (age) input to a date input with `min_age: 18` validation. Section key and question key stay the same to preserve conditional logic and existing submissions.

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `gorocky-ed-assessment` end-to-end — confirm the GoRocky navbar appears on landing, consent, nitrates, and contact-details screens (but NOT on the landing / confidence transition / personalized-plan screens)
- [ ] Reach the medical-conditions section — confirm the "Your data is encrypted" strip renders
- [ ] On any question screen, confirm Continue is disabled until a required field is filled, and that clicking Back correctly returns to the previous section (landing/consent included)
- [ ] Confirm question screens with empty prompts (e.g. `treatment_preference`) render **without** a stray asterisk
- [ ] Run `gofuller-assessment` and confirm the former "Age Verification" step now reads "Date of Birth" and accepts a date input
- [ ] Spot-check brand theming: GoFuller hover = teal, GoSlim hover = yellow, GoRocky hover = orange (no hardcoded orange leaking through)
- [ ] Run the migration: `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate`

## Migrations

```bash
pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate
```

Rolls back with the matching file in `rollback/`.

Most changes are small but they add up to a much more coherent flow: consistent brand theming, no more orphan asterisks or duplicate question text, proper back navigation, and the new GoRock

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/qr landing v2
GoRocky/core.description: # Add QR landing page (/welcome-v2) with split desktop layout ## Summary Introduces a new public `/welcome-v2` route — a QR-code landing page that pairs a branded marketing panel with the signup form on desktop, and a mobile-optimized single-column layout with a floating CTA. ## Changes ### New route - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/welcome-v2/page.tsx` — QR landing page with responsive mobile + desktop layouts - `apps/web/src/app/(public)/welcome-v2/source-tracker.tsx` — signup source cookie tracker - `apps/web/next.config.ts` — allowlist new CDN host for hero image ### Mobile layout - Full-bleed hero image anchored to the bottom of the viewport - Sticky/floating CTA button with `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` for iPhone home-indicator respect - Blur-gradient overlay (`from-ed-400/80 to-ed-600/80 blur-[50px]`) fades the bottom of the hero into the CTA - Vertical brand gradient background (`#FBEDE6 → #DC6B3B → #D85328`) that starts solid cream before transitioning ### Desktop layout - Two-column split: marketing panel (left) + signup form (right) - Hero image centered horizontally within the left panel - Same blur-gradient treatment as mobile (replaced the previous `feGaussianBlur` SVG) - Scrolling benefits marquee (Faster Reordering · Skip the Form · Order Tracking) ### UX / polish - Subtle text-shadow on tagline headings (`0px 2px 4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.12)`) - UTM passthrough (`utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign`, `utm_content`, `utm_term`) forwarded to `/auth/signup-flow2` - Analytics: tracks `create_account_cta` click with `flushSync` (sendBeacon) so events survive navigation - Source cookie set to `qr-landing` for downstream attribution ## Test plan - [ ] Visit `/welcome-v2` on mobile — hero fills the viewport, CTA floats above the blur gradient, no overlap with the app footer - [ ] Visit `/welcome-v2` on desktop — left panel shows marketing content and hero, right panel shows the signup form - [ ] Append `?utm_source=qr&utm_campaign=test` — confirm params pass through to the signup-flow2 page - [ ] Click the CTA — confirm `create_account_cta` event fires and navigation succeeds - [ ] Verify signup source cookie (`qr-landing`) is set on page load - [ ] Test on iPhone with home indicator — CTA padding clears the indicator area
GoRocky/core.summary: Introduces a new public `/welcome-v2` route — a QR-code landing page that pairs a branded marketing panel with the signup form on desktop, and a mobile-optimized single-column layout with a floating CTA.

GoRocky/core.title: Update ED prices and add dosage selector in ED product catalog page
GoRocky/core.description: # PR: ED Order Page — Shopify Checkout Links, Pricing Updates & Dosage Selector ## Summary Wires up all ED product cards on `/order/gorocky` to real Shopify checkout URLs, updates pricing to match the latest product catalog, and adds a dosage dropdown for multi-dosage products (Viagra). --- ## Changes ### Shopify Checkout Links All product variants now link directly to Shopify checkout — no more placeholder `#` URLs. The "Buy Now" button redirects to the correct Shopify cart for the selected variant. ### Pricing Updates Updated prices to match the latest product catalog: | Product | Variants | Prices | |---------|----------|--------| | **Sildenafil 50mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 / 24 pills | ₱890 / ₱1,290 / ₱1,890 / ₱2,890 | | **Sildenafil 100mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 / 24 pills | ₱990 / ₱1,390 / ₱1,990 / ₱2,990 | | **ED Power Bundle** | 12 / 24 pills | ₱3,990 / ₱4,990 | | **Tadalafil 20mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 pills | ₱1,690 / ₱3,290 / ₱4,490 | | **Viagra 25mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 pcs | ₱5,390 / ₱9,990 / ₱13,990 | | **Viagra 50mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 pcs | ₱7,390 / ₱13,990 / ₱20,990 | | **Viagra 100mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 pcs | ₱8,390 / ₱14,990 / ₱21,390 | | **Cialis 20mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 pcs | ₱7,490 / ₱13,390 / ₱19,490 | ### Dosage Selector for Multi-Dosage Products Added a `dosage` field to `ProductVariant` and a two-dropdown UX for products with multiple dosages (currently Viagra): - **"Select a Dosage"** dropdown — filters available quantities by dosage (25mg / 50mg / 100mg) - **"Select a Quantity"** dropdown — shows only quantities for the selected dosage - Changing dosage resets quantity to the "Most Popular" option within that dosage group ### Smart Default Selection Dropdowns now auto-select the variant tagged with `badgeLabel: "Most Popular"` on initial render, rather than always defaulting to the first option. This applies to both single-dropdown and two-dropdown products. ### Out-of-Stock Products Hidden Out-of-stock products (Tadalafil Strips) are now filtered out of the grid entirely instead of rendering a disabled card, so remaining cards fill the space without gaps. ### Card Layout — Aligned Benefits Reordered card sections so benefits appear at a consistent vertical position across all cards, regardless of whether they have 1 or 2 dropdowns. Layout order: product info → price badge → benefits → variant selectors → spacer → total price → Buy Now → rating. ### Bug Fixes (from PR review) - Fixed `text-md` → `text-base` across GoslimHero, GoslimPackages, GoslimProcess, and Header mobile nav - Restored `max-[991px]:h-16` responsive class on Header spacer div - Added `mb-0` margin reset to GoslimCareTeam `h3` --- ## Files Changed | File | Change | |------|--------| | `apps/webflow/src/components/order/WebflowProductCard.tsx` | Dosage dropdown, smart defaults, aligned layout, hide out-of-stock | | `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/page.tsx` | All checkout URLs, updated pricing, dosage data for Viagra | | `apps/webflow/src/components/layout/Header.tsx` | `text-md` fix, spacer responsive class | | `apps/webflow/src/components/treatments/goslim/*` | `text-md`, `mb-0`, and spacing fixes | | `apps/webflow/src/components/treatments/gorocky/GorockyProcess.tsx` | Merge conflict resolution + `font-radio-canada` |
GoRocky/core.summary: Wires up all ED product cards on `/order/gorocky` to real Shopify checkout URLs, updates pricing to match the latest product catalog, and adds a dosage dropdown for multi-dosage products (Viagra). --

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/webflow ed catalog
GoRocky/core.description: # PR: Webflow ED Product Catalog — New Card Component & Shopify Checkout Links ## Summary Migrates the GoRocky ED order page (`/order/gorocky`) to a new `WebflowProductCard` component with structured variant data and wires up all products to their actual Shopify checkout URLs. --- ## What Changed ### New Component: `WebflowProductCard` A new client-side product card component (`components/order/WebflowProductCard.tsx`) purpose-built for the webflow marketing site: - **Variant selector** — Dropdown that dynamically updates the displayed price and "Buy Now" link based on selected variant - **Per-unit pricing** — Automatically computes and displays the lowest per-tablet price across all variants - **Badges** — Supports "Recommended", custom badges (e.g. "First in PH"), and per-variant badges ("Most Popular", "Best Value") - **Out of stock** — Graceful handling when `variants` is empty or `outOfStock` is set - **Ratings** — Star rating with review count and sold count ### Order Page Migration Replaced the old `OrderProductCard`/shared component system with the new `WebflowProductCard`. The page now uses a `ProductCardData` interface with typed `ProductVariant[]` instead of generic option group dropdowns. ### Shopify Checkout Links All 7 products now have real Shopify checkout URLs mapped to each variant: | Product | Variants | Checkout | |---------|----------|----------| | **Sildenafil 50mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 / 24 pills | Shopify one-time | | **Sildenafil 100mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 / 24 pills | Shopify one-time | | **ED Power Bundle** | Performance Duo (12) / Power Play (24) | Shopify one-time | | **Tadalafil 20mg** | 4 / 8 / 12 pills | Shopify one-time | | **Tadalafil Strips** | — | Out of Stock | | **Viagra** | 25mg/50mg/100mg × 4/8/12 pcs (9 variants) | Shopify one-time | | **Cialis** | 4 / 8 / 12 pcs | Shopify one-time | --- ## Files Changed **2 files** — ~418 additions, ~140 deletions | File | Change | |------|--------| | `apps/webflow/src/components/order/WebflowProductCard.tsx` | **New** — Product card component with variant selection | | `apps/webflow/src/app/order/gorocky/page.tsx` | Migrated to new component, added all Shopify checkout URLs and correct pricing |
GoRocky/core.summary: Migrates the GoRocky ED order page (`/order/gorocky`) to a new `WebflowProductCard` component with structured variant data and wires up all products to their actual Shopify checkout URLs. --

Created a reusable `FeaturedOnSection` component (press logos) and added it to both the homepage and GoRocky treatments page - Simplified data structures across all sections by removing unnecessary TypeScript interfaces and reducing complexity - Replaced lucide icon imports with inline SVGs to match Webflow's icon style ## Changes by section ### Homepage (`apps/web`) | Section | Changes | |---|---| | **HomeCategoriesSection** | Adopted webflow card structure with `id` field, `inline-grid`, cleaned up class ordering, mobile image width `w-32` | | **FeaturedOnSection** | New reusable component extracted from the WL consultation landing page | | **WhatIsGoRockySection** | Replaced desktop/mobile image card system with simpler webflow layout, updated R2 links to `WG-1.a/b/c.webp` | | **DoctorsSection** | Unified card gradient (`from-[#d64f00] to-[#471703]`), responsive carousel with `useCardsPerPage`, updated doctor images | | **ReviewsSection** | Radial gradient background, page-based carousel, inline SVG stars, R2 verified badge, simplified review data | | **BlogArticlesSection** | Flat tab/posts structure, unified active tab color, responsive grid (1-col mobile, 3-col desktop), updated R2 blog images | | **InvestorsSection** | `flex-wrap justify-center` layout for responsive rows (1 row desktop, 2 rows sm, 3 rows xs) | ### GoRocky Treatments Page (`apps/web`) | Section | Changes | |---|---| | **HeroSection** | Overlay image positioning, inline SVG icons, percentage-based spacing, Reviews.io logo | | **PackagesSection** | Webflow card layout with `rounded-br-[40px]`, inline SVG check icons, gradient CTA buttons | | **ExpertCareSection** | Separate mobile (stacked) / desktop (overlay) layouts, updated doctor image, CSS variable gradient | | **FeaturedOnSection** | Added after PackagesSection | ### Other - **Footer**: Adjusted disclaimer text styling - **Header**: Updated logo sizing and spacing - Minor formatting fixes in `apps/webflow` blog and doctor sections ## Files changed - **21 files** modified across `apps/web` and `apps/webflow` - **1 new file**: `apps/web/src/components/landing-page/FeaturedOnSection.tsx` ## Screenshots (Before and After) ![Uploading image.png…]() ### Added "As Featured On" component to both home page and ED treatemtents page ## Test plan - [ ] Verify homepage renders all sections correctly on mobile, tablet, and desktop - [ ] Verify GoRocky treatments page renders correctly across breakpoints - [ ] Check that all R2 images load (doctor photos, blog thumbnails, press logos, investor logos) - GoRocky/core.title: Feature/webflow migration
GoRocky/core.description:
# PR Summary
 
## Overview
 
This PR brings the first major `feature/webflow-migration` rollout into `main` by migrating core Webflow-driven marketing pages into the Next.js web app, consolidating design assets, and expanding content and product surfaces across brands.
 
## What’s included
 
- **Webflow to Next.js migration**
  - Migrates the main landing experience into reusable app components
  - Ports key treatment and marketing sections out of legacy Webflow pages
  - Removes old Webflow HTML files that are no longer needed in the web app flow
 
- **New and updated marketing pages**
  - Added or converted:
    - home and landing page sections
    - About page
    - legal-related pages
    - blog listing, detail, and category pages
    - product info pages
    - order pages for GoFuller, GoRocky, and GoSlim
    - eKonsulta landing content and supporting sections
 
- **Treatment page migration**
  - Converted major treatment pages and components for:
    - GoRocky
    - GoSlim
    - GoFuller
  - Added or reworked supporting sections like hero, packages, process, reviews, doctor highlights, and educational sections
 
- **Design system and asset cleanup**
  - Added shared global styling variables for the design system
  - Updated navbar and footer components
  - Replaced local image references with R2-hosted assets where needed
  - Added and organized new landing and treatment page imagery
  - Updated `.gitignore` for Webflow source artifacts
 
- **Content and UX updates**
  - Header and navigation updates, including treatment dropdown improvements
  - Footer cleanup
  - Blog link updates to point to local blog pages instead of legacy Webflow links
  - Various copy and layout refinements across migrated pages
 
- **Infra and migration support**
  - Added Webflow staging app scaffolding and related migration utilities and documentation
  - Included fixes for static asset handling and deployment-related Webflow issues
 
## Notes
 
- This is a large migration PR and includes both page conversion work and supporting cleanup needed to make the migrated experience usable in the main app.
- The branch history also includes a few merged fixes from other workstreams already pulled into this branch, including security and role-management related fixes and general staging sync work.
 
## Suggested testing
 
- Verify main marketing pages render correctly across breakpoints
- Check treatment pages for GoRocky, GoSlim, and GoFuller
- Check blog listing, category, and detail routes
- Verify product info and order pages load correctly
- Confirm navbar and footer behavior and navigation links
- Validate remote images and assets load correctly in production builds
 
## Short version
 
**Summary**
- Migrate core Webflow marketing pages and sections into the Next.js web app
- Add and convert treatment, blog, about, legal, product info, and order pages
- Introduce shared navbar, footer, and design-system styling updates
- Move assets to cleaner R2-backed references and remove legacy Webflow HTML dependencies
 
**Why**
- Reduce reliance on legacy Webflow-rendered pages
- Move toward reusable app-native components
- Make marketing and product pages easier to maintain and extend in the main web app
 
**Testing**
- Smoke test landing pages, treatment pages, blog routes, product info pages, order pages, and shared navigation and footer
GoRocky/core.summary: This PR brings the first major `feature/webflow-migration` rollout into `main` by migrating core Webflow-driven marketing pages into the Next.js web app, consolidating design assets, and expanding content and product surfaces across brands. - **Webflow to Next.js migration** - Migrates the main land
