GoRocky/core.title: Feature/ed assessment v2
GoRocky/core.description:
# Form UI Improvements: Consistent Width and Alignment

## Summary
This PR implements consistent width constraints and improved alignment across all form components, creating a more polished and professional user experience for the GoRocky ED assessment forms.

## Changes Made

### 1. Consistent Width Constraints (`max-w-lg`)
Applied uniform `max-w-lg` (512px) width constraint across all form elements:

- **Radio button groups** ([RadioButtonGroup.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/fields/RadioButtonGroup.tsx:0:0-0:0))
  - Added `max-w-lg mx-auto` to center and constrain button groups
  
- **Form inputs** ([QuestionRenderer.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/QuestionRenderer.tsx:0:0-0:0))
  - Wrapped question prompts, descriptions, inputs, and error messages in `max-w-lg mx-auto` containers
  - Created reusable `contentWidthClassName` constant for consistency
  
- **Continue button** ([FormContinueButton.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/FormContinueButton.tsx:0:0-0:0))
  - Wrapped button in `max-w-lg mx-auto` container
  - Removed redundant width classes from button itself
  
- **Section descriptions** ([SectionHeader.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/SectionHeader.tsx:0:0-0:0))
  - Applied `max-w-lg mx-auto` to section description text
  
- **Summary items** ([EDSummarySection.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/ed/EDSummarySection.tsx:0:0-0:0))
  - Constrained summary cards to `max-w-lg mx-auto`

### 2. Layout Improvements

#### Column Layout
- Changed radio button groups from 2-column to 1-column layout for better readability
- Updated [RadioButtonGroup](cci:1://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/fields/RadioButtonGroup.tsx:32:0-161:1) columns prop from dynamic to fixed `columns={1}`

#### Text Alignment
- Changed section descriptions and question prompts from center-aligned to left-aligned
- Updated [SectionHeader.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/SectionHeader.tsx:0:0-0:0): removed `text-center` from description
- Updated [QuestionRenderer.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/QuestionRenderer.tsx:0:0-0:0): changed from `justify-center text-center` to `justify-left text-left`

#### Date Input Enhancement
- Moved the Manual/Spinner toggle switch to the same row as the question description
- Prevented duplicate description rendering for date inputs
- Improved visual hierarchy and space efficiency

### 3. Marketing Consent Checkbox
- Added optional marketing consent checkbox to `GoRockyConsentPage.tsx`
- Includes state management and form data integration
- Styled to match existing consent UI with orange accent color
- Stores consent in `marketing_consent` field

### 4. Landing Page Updates (`GoRockyLandingPage.tsx`)
- Simplified component to use `CustomComponentProps` interface
- Removed theme prop complexity
- Added "Already have an account? Log in here" link
- Added `unoptimized` prop to checkmark images

## Files Modified

### Frontend Components
- [apps/web/src/components/forms/fields/RadioButtonGroup.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/fields/RadioButtonGroup.tsx:0:0-0:0)
- [apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/QuestionRenderer.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/QuestionRenderer.tsx:0:0-0:0)
- [apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/FormContinueButton.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/FormContinueButton.tsx:0:0-0:0)
- [apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/SectionHeader.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/SectionHeader.tsx:0:0-0:0)
- [apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/ed/EDSummarySection.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/core/apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/ed/EDSummarySection.tsx:0:0-0:0)
- `apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/ed/GoRockyConsentPage.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/components/forms/custom/ed/GoRockyLandingPage.tsx`

### Backend (Database)
- `apps/backend/src/database/seeds/025_gorocky_ed_assessment_v2.sql`
  - Updated ED duration question from multiple-choice to Yes/No format
  - Changed prompt to "Have you had trouble with having an erection for more than 6 months?"

## Visual Impact

**Before:**
- Inconsistent widths between form elements
- Continue button wider than inputs
- Section descriptions spanning full width
- Radio buttons in 2-column layout
- Center-aligned text throughout

**After:**
- All elements aligned with consistent `max-w-lg` width
- Clean, centered layout with proper spacing
- Single-column radio buttons for better readability
- Left-aligned text for improved scannability
- Professional, cohesive appearance

## Testing Recommendations

- [ ] Test form rendering on mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
- [ ] Verify all radio button groups display in single column
- [ ] Confirm continue button matches width of form inputs
- [ ] Check date input switch appears on same row as description
- [ ] Validate marketing consent checkbox functionality
- [ ] Test form submission with new marketing consent field

## Breaking Changes
None - all changes are purely visual/UI improvements

## Related Issues
Addresses UI consistency and alignment issues in the ED assessment form flow
GoRocky/core.summary: This PR implements consistent width constraints and improved alignment across all form components, creating a more polished and professional user experience for the GoRocky ED assessment forms.

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/webflow migration
GoRocky/core.description:
# PR Summary: Webflow Migration of Static Pages to Next.js + Tailwind

## Overview

Full migration of the GoRocky marketing website from Webflow to a Next.js 15 app (`apps/webflow/`) within the monorepo. All pages have been converted from Webflow HTML/CSS to React components with Tailwind CSS, with images served from Cloudflare R2 CDN.

**664 files changed** | **142,035 insertions** | **21,128 deletions**

---

## New App: `apps/webflow/`

Standalone Next.js 15 app for the public marketing site, fully decoupled from the patient-facing `apps/web/`.

### Pages Converted

#### Home Page (`/`)
- Hero section with CTA
- Treatment categories grid (3-column)
- Doctor carousel (responsive: 1/3/4/5 cards per breakpoint with auto-play + pagination)
- Patient reviews carousel
- Investor logos section
- About section

#### Treatment Pages (4 pages)
- `/treatments/gorocky` — ED treatments (hero, packages, process, doctor feature)
- `/treatments/gofuller` — Hair loss treatments (hero, packages with gradient CTAs, process, reviews carousel with testimonial images + plan info + star ratings + Reviews.io branding)
- `/treatments/goslim` — Weight loss treatments (hero, packages, process, BMI calculator, care team, compounded section, before/after testimonials, doctor feature, UVP)
- `/treatments/govax` — Vaccination page (hero, stats, options, doses, protection reasons, partners)

#### About Pages (5 pages)
- `/about` — About Us page with 6 section components (hero, who we are, treatments, milestones, founders, CTA)
- `/about/privacy-policy` — Data Privacy Policy
- `/about/return-and-refund-policy` — Return and Refund Policy
- `/about/terms-and-conditions` — Terms and Conditions
- `/about/informed-consent-gorocky` — Telemedicine Informed Consent

#### Blog System (3 page types)
- `/blog` — Blog listing with category filter (All / Erectile Dysfunction / GoVax / Hair Loss / Premature Ejaculation / Weight Loss)
- `/blog/[slug]` — Dynamic blog detail pages with static generation, breadcrumbs, key takeaways, rich text content, inline CTAs, references accordion, doctor profile, editorial standards, related posts
- `/blog-category/[category]` — Blog category landing pages (sexual-health, hair-health, weight-management) with subcategory filters, dynamic post grid

#### Ad Landing Pages
- `/meta/treatments/gofuller` — Meta/Facebook GoFuller landing
- `/meta/treatments/gorocky` — Meta/Facebook GoRocky landing
- `/meta/treatments/goslim` — Meta/Facebook GoSlim landing
- `/tiktok/treatments/goslim` — TikTok GoSlim landing

---

## Shared Layout Components

- **Header** — Fixed navbar with blog dropdown (Sexual Health / Hair Health / Weight Management), About Us, Help Center, Sign In. Responsive with hamburger menu at ≤ 991px
- **Footer** — 4-column layout (brand + socials + certificates, company links, blog links, contact cards). Internal routing for all converted pages, external links for non-migrated pages
- **GR Logo** — Reusable GoRocky logo component
- **ReviewsioLogo** — Inline SVG Reviews.io branding component
- **StarRating** — Reusable star rating display

---

## Blog Data System

- `blog-types.ts` — BlogPost interface with 30+ fields (content, metadata, CTAs, images, dates, flags)
- `blog-posts.ts` — Full blog post dataset (100+ posts) parsed from Webflow CMS CSV export
- `blog-categories.ts` — Category page config mapping URL slugs to post categories and subcategory filters
- `parse-blog-csv.ts` — Script to re-import from Webflow CMS CSV exports
- 13 blog components (BlogCard, CategoryFilter, SubcategoryFilter, Breadcrumb, BlogCTA, BottomCTA, DoctorProfile, EditorialStandards, KeyTakeaways, ReferencesAccordion, RelatedBlogs, RichTextContent)

---

## Design System

- `globals.css` — CSS variables for brand colors per treatment (ED: `--color-ed-*`, HL: `--color-hl-*`, WL: `--color-wl-*`), GoRocky brand orange (`#d64f00`), typography (Radio Canada, Montserrat)
- `gorocky.css` — Brand-specific style overrides
- All styling via Tailwind CSS 4 — zero dependency on Webflow CSS files for converted pages

---

## Image Optimization

- All component images served from **Cloudflare R2 CDN** (`https://pub-9c9b723670544e3e92cab6cef84a4267.r2.dev/`)
- **Removed 58 redundant local images** that were duplicates of R2-hosted files
- Remaining local images are either favicons/webclips or legacy Webflow assets not yet referenced in code

---

## Navigation Updates

- Header blog dropdown links updated from external `gorocky.ph` URLs to internal `/blog-category/*` routes
- Footer blog links updated to internal routes with `target="_blank"` removed
- Footer company links updated: Blog → `/blog`, About Us → `/about`, legal pages → `/about/*`
- About Us link in header (desktop + mobile) updated to `/about`

---

## Infrastructure

- `railway.webflow.toml` — Railway deployment config for the webflow app
- `next.config.ts` — Image domains configured for R2 CDN and Webflow CDN
- Webflow CSS files retained in `public/css/` for any remaining unconverted HTML reference pages
GoRocky/core.summary: Full migration of the GoRocky marketing website from Webflow to a Next.js 15 app (`apps/webflow/`) within the monorepo. All pages have been converted from Webflow HTML/CSS to React components with Tailwind CSS, with images served from Cloudflare R2 CDN. **664 files changed** | **142,035 insertions**

GoRocky/core.title: Feature/webflow migration
GoRocky/core.description:
# Commits (chronological)
1. Initialize webflow staging app — Scaffolded the Next.js app with Tailwind, ESLint, and imported the original Webflow CSS/JS assets as reference files.

2. Convert webflow landing page — Built the homepage as React components: Hero, Categories, About, Doctors, Reviews, Influencers, Investors, Blogs, and Footer sections.

3. Document conversion status & add treatment pages — Created treatment pages for GoRocky (ED), GoFuller (HL), GoSlim (WL), and GoVax (Vaccines), plus Meta and TikTok ad landing variants. Added a CONVERSION_STATUS.md tracker.

4. Add blog detail page & CSV import script — Built the blog listing and detail pages with components (BlogCard, CategoryFilter, KeyTakeaways, DoctorProfile, RichTextContent, etc.) and a CSV-to-JSON blog import script.

5. Add blog system listing/detail — Finalized the blog system with data types and content structure.

6. Add landing page images — Imported all homepage images (logos, doctor photos, category cards, trust badges).

7. Add treatment page images — Imported treatment-specific images (before/after photos, product shots, hero images, icons).

8. Add eKonsulta landing page — Built the GoSlim x eKonsulta partner page with hero, doctors, testimonials, why-join, header, and footer components plus a StarRating UI component.

# Key areas created
- Pages: Homepage, 4 treatment pages, 3 Meta ad pages, 1 TikTok ad page, blog listing/detail, eKonsulta partner page
- Components: ~30+ React components across layout, sections, blog, and partner directories
- Assets: Large batch of marketing images in public/images/
- Tooling: Blog CSV parser script, conversion status tracker

# Created Reusable Form Components and Test UI Page [WIP]

## Summary
Built a comprehensive set of reusable form components with consistent styling and a test page demonstrating their usage. This includes phone input with country selection, location dropdowns with cascading filters, and an onboarding step card component.

## Changes

### New Components
- **PhoneInput** (`phone-number-input.tsx`)
  - Country selector with flag display and calling code
  - Searchable country dropdown using Command component
  - Consistent height and styling with other inputs
  - Full-width responsive layout

- **Select** [select-location.tsx]
  - Custom select component matching Input design system
  - Floating label support (h-14, border-2, rounded-lg)
  - Chevron icon with rotation animation
  - Built for cascading location dropdowns

- **StepCard** [step-card.tsx]
  - Reusable onboarding step component
  - Displays step number badge, title, description, and image
  - Optional `isActive` prop for highlighting current step
  - Responsive image sizing with full-height support

### Component Updates
- **Input** (`input.tsx`)
  - Fixed className application to wrapper div instead of input element
  - Enables proper flex-1 behavior in composite components like PhoneInput
  - Maintains floating label functionality

- **Button** (`button.tsx`)
  - Restored custom styling with CSS variable-based theming
  - Maintains GoRocky brand colors

### Dependencies
- Added shadcn/ui components: Command, Popover, ScrollArea, Dialog
- Required for PhoneInput country selector functionality

### Test Page
- **Test UI Page** ([app/test-ui/page.tsx]
  - Welcome section with personalized greeting
  - Three-step onboarding cards explaining the process
  - OTP verification section with resend link
  - Personal details form (first/last name, email, phone)
  - Address details with cascading selects (province → city → barangay)
  - Pre-filled form data for testing
  - Proper form validation and state management

## Technical Details
- All components follow Airbnb Style Guide and TypeScript best practices
- Consistent design system: h-14 inputs, rounded-lg borders, floating labels
- Accessible markup with proper ARIA attributes
- Responsive layouts with Tailwind CSS
- Cascading dropdown logic with filtered options

## Testing
- Manually tested all form components in test UI page
- Verified cascading dropdown behavior (province → city → barangay)
- Confirmed phone input country selector functionality
- Validated responsive layouts and styling consistency
GoRocky/core.summary: Built a comprehensive set of reusable form components with consistent styling and a test page demonstrating their usage. This includes phone input with country selection, location dropdowns with cascading filters, and an onboarding step card component.

GoRocky/aperture.title: Refactor/quality of life improvements
GoRocky/aperture.description: # Quality of Life Improvements Branch: `refactor/quality-of-life-improvements` → `main` ## Summary A handful of UX upgrades that make folder organization and asset management feel native. The headline change is end-to-end nested folder support — backend already had `parent_id` on `folders`, but the UI didn't expose it. This PR finishes the feature and bundles in a few small wins (keyboard shortcuts, confirm modals, empty-state polish). ## What's new ### Nested folders - **Parent picker in "New Folder" form** — pick where the folder lives at creation time. Defaults to the currently-viewed folder, so creating from inside one nests naturally. - **Hover-only `+` and trash gear buttons on every folder row** — quick "new subfolder here" and "configure this folder," no menu digging required. - **Drag-to-reparent with positional intent** — dropping in the *middle* of a row reparents into that folder; dropping near the *top* or *bottom* edge inserts as a sibling above/below. Visual indicators (blue ring vs. blue line) preview the action before release. - **Root drop zone** — drop a folder onto "All Assets" to move it back to root. - **Breadcrumbs above the asset grid** — `All Assets / hl / subs / oral-sku` rendered for nested paths, each segment clickable. Replaces the old "Folder: {id}" filter pill. - **Subfolder tiles** — child folders surface as clickable tiles at the top of the asset grid when viewing a folder. - **Guards** — can't drop a folder onto itself or any descendant (would orphan the subtree); cycle prevention is enforced client-side before the API call. ### Folder deletion - **Trash icon on hover** with a confirm dialog. Cascades subfolders, but assets fall through to "unfiled" rather than being deleted — recoverable. - **Settings modal danger zone** — typed-name confirmation for the same operation when accessed via the settings UI. Renders via portal. - If you delete the folder you're currently viewing, you're auto-navigated back to All Assets. ### Asset selection: bulk delete with previews - **`DeleteConfirmModal`** — renders up to 6 thumbnail previews of what's about to be deleted, async confirm with busy state, Esc to cancel. ### Keyboard shortcuts - **`/`** — focuses the search input (skipped when already typing in a field). Search placeholder hints this. Esc blurs the search box. - **`cmd/ctrl + A`** — selects all assets in the current view. - **`Esc`** — clears the current asset selection. - **`Delete` / `Backspace`** — opens the delete-confirm modal for selected assets. - All shortcuts ignore key events when focus is in an input, textarea, select, or contenteditable element. ### Empty state polish - `EmptyState` now distinguishes between "no assets at all" and "no matches for current filters," giving a more accurate message when filters are active. ## Architecture notes - **No schema changes** — the `folders` table already had `parent_id UUID REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE` and the `/api/folders` endpoints already accepted/returned it. This PR is entirely UI work. - **Asset URLs are stable across folder moves.** Moving an asset between folders only updates the `folder_id` column on the DB row; the S3 key (and CDN URL) stays the same. URLs embedded in external content keep working forever, and moves are instant/free (no S3 copy/delete cost). - **Drop intent uses cursor Y, not the dragged item's center.** A window-level `pointermove` listener tracks `clientY`; in `handleDragOver`, we compute the cursor's relative position within the over rect (`< 0.2` → above, `> 0.8` → below, else into). This was reliable across row sizes; using the dragged item's center made the "into" zone hard to hit. - **Position uses fractional indexing** (`fractional-indexing` package) — moves and reorders only ever update the moved row's `position`, never neighboring rows. ## Files changed | File | What | |---|---| | `folder-tree.tsx` | Parent picker, hover `+`/trash, delete handler | | `sortable-folder-tree.tsx` | Same as above + drag-to-reparent with positional intent, root drop zone | | `folder-breadcrumbs.tsx` *(new)* | Walks folder tree to render path segments | | `subfolder-tiles.tsx` *(new)* | Clickable child-folder tiles above asset grid | | `delete-confirm-modal.tsx` *(new)* | Bulk delete confirm with previews | | `folder-settings-modal.tsx` | Danger zone with typed-name confirm | | `empty-state.tsx` | "No matches" variant when filters are active | | `dashboard/page.tsx` | Wires breadcrumbs + subfolder tiles, removes folder filter pill | | `top-nav.tsx` | `/` shortcut to focus search, Esc to blur | | `sortable-asset-grid.tsx` | `cmd+A` / `Esc` / `Delete` shortcut handling | | `type-filter.tsx` | Minor styling adjustments | ## Test plan - [x] Create a folder at root → appears in sidebar - [x] Create a folder while viewing another folder → new folder is nested as a child - [x] Click `+` on a folder row → form opens with that folder pre-selected as parent - [x] Drag folder onto another folder's *middle* → reparents (blue ring shows during drag) - [x] Drag folder onto another folder's *top edge* → inserts as sibling above (blue line shows) - [x] Drag folder onto another folder's *bottom edge* → inserts as sibling below - [x] Drag folder onto "All Assets" → moves to root - [x] Drag folder onto itself or a descendant → no-op (no orphaning) - [x] Delete a folder with subfolders → confirm message mentions subfolders; assets land in unfiled - [x] Delete the folder you're currently viewing → navigated to All Assets - [x] Navigate into nested folder → breadcrumbs render full path; each segment is clickable - [x] Press `/` anywhere outside an input → search input focuses - [x] Press `cmd+A` while viewing assets → all selected; `Esc` clears - [x] Select assets, press `Delete` → confirm modal appears with thumbnails - [x] Asset URLs unchanged after moving an asset between folders --- Need help on this PR? Tag @codesmith with what you need. - [ ] Let Codesmith autofix CI failures and bot reviews

The headline change is end-to-end nested folder support — backend already had `parent_id` on `folders`, but the UI didn't expose it. This PR finishes the feature and bundles in a few small wins (keyboard shortcu

GoRocky/goforms.title: Feat/wl oral SKU
GoRocky/goforms.description: # PR: feat/wl-oral-sku — Add Oral Weight Loss SKUs (Orlistat & Oral Compounded Semaglutide) ## Overview Introduces two new oral weight loss products — **Orlistat** and **Oral Compounded Semaglutide** — as fully supported SKUs within the GoSlim/GoRocky personalized plan flow. Both products are available via WL bypass links and are surfaced as cross-sell options on injectable product pages. --- ## What Changed ### New Products - **Orlistat** — oral fat-absorption inhibitor, starting at ₱2,490/month - Variants: 30-capsule (1-month) and 90-capsule (3-month) supply - Dedicated FAQs, hero images, and thumbnail gallery - **Oral Compounded Semaglutide** — oral GLP-1, starting at ₱14,990/month - Variants: 3mg (starting), 7mg (mid), 14mg (advanced) - Dedicated FAQs, hero images, and thumbnail gallery ### Product Recommendation UI (`ProductRecommendation.tsx`) - Extended variant picker to support oral product variant arrays (`ORAL_SEMAGLUTIDE_VARIANTS`, `ORLISTAT_VARIANTS`) alongside existing injectable variants - Added `getActiveVariants`, `getSelectedVariant`, `getVariantSublabel`, and `getVariantDetail` helpers to dispatch the correct variant data per product type - Oral products show a cross-sell section listing injectable options; injectable products show an oral product upsell section (previously only showed other injectables) - Oral products are excluded from the injectable eligible products list to prevent cross-contamination - Delivery text is now conditional: oral products show **"Free discreet delivery nationwide"**; injectables show **"Nationwide discreet delivery (Free within Metro Manila)"** - Orlistat variant picker hides the duration detail line (capsule count is self-explanatory) ### WL Bypass Flow (`FormRenderer.tsx`) - Expanded the bypass parameter map to support `orlistat` and `oral-semaglutide` in addition to existing injectable keys - Oral bypass products skip the PlanSelection step and go directly to Shopify checkout (injectable products already did this; oral products now follow the same path) - Eligible products seeded during bypass are now product-type-aware: oral bypasses seed oral alternatives, injectable bypasses seed injectable alternatives ### Product Data (`recommendation-products.ts`) - Added full product records for `orlistat` and `oral-compounded-semaglutide` with images, FAQs, pricing, and checkout URLs - Added `ORAL_SEMAGLUTIDE_VARIANTS` and `ORLISTAT_VARIANTS` constant arrays with per-variant Shopify checkout URLs keyed by form slug - Introduced the `OralVariantOption` interface (a unified type shared by all variant pickers) - Renamed injectable package names from "Weight Loss Package" to "Weight Management Package" for consistency - Added `startingMonthlyPriceOverride` to Compounded Semaglutide to surface a discounted per-month price ### Assets - Added product image sets (hero, lifestyle, product-only) for both Orlistat and Oral Compounded Semaglutide under `public/images/products/orlistat/` and `public/images/products/oral-semag/` in 720px, 1080px, and 1440px variants --- ## Testing Checklist - [x] WL bypass URL with `?wl=orlistat` routes directly to ProductRecommendation and skips PlanSelection - [x] WL bypass URL with `?wl=oral-semaglutide` does the same - [x] Orlistat variant picker displays 30-cap and 90-cap options with correct pricing - [x] Oral Semaglutide variant picker displays 3mg/7mg/14mg with correct sublabels and pricing - [x] Selecting an oral product shows injectable cross-sell section below - [x] Selecting an injectable product shows oral product upsell section below - [x] Delivery text reads "Free discreet delivery nationwide" on oral product pages - [x] Delivery text reads "Nationwide discreet delivery (Free within Metro Manila)" on injectable product pages - [x] CTA from oral product pages resolves to correct Shopify checkout URL Accessible via query parameter like: /forms/gorocky-wl-assessment-v2?**wl_bypass=orlistat**
GoRocky/goforms.summary: Introduces two new oral weight loss products — **Orlistat** and **Oral Compounded Semaglutide** — as fully supported SKUs within the GoSlim/GoRocky personalized plan flow. Both products are available via WL bypass links and are surfaced as cross-sell options on injectable product pages. --- - **Orli

GoRocky/goforms.title: Feature/GoRocky WL trial pack assessment
GoRocky/goforms.description: # PR: WL Trial Pack Assessment Flow ## Summary - **New WL Trial Pack Assessment Flow**: End-to-end trial pack assessment with landing page, personal details collection, consent, eligibility checks, and trial pack selection — all branded with GoRocky ED styling - **Trial Pack Selection Page**: Full product page with hero, product card, inclusions (with info tooltip for lab tests), trial experience steps, FAQ, testimonials, impact stats, customer support, and a scroll-triggered sticky checkout footer - **Reusable EligibleProductsList Component**: Extracted from `ProductRecommendation` into a shared component, used in both the trial pack and full product recommendation views - **FormLayoutWithImage Mobile Fix**: Switched mobile image from fixed-height `fill` to intrinsic `w-full h-auto` sizing so images aren't clipped on varying screen sizes - **Database Migrations**: Two new migrations to clone the GoRocky WL trial assessment form and update custom component names from GoSlim to WLTrial ## Key Changes - **New pages**: `WLTrialLandingPage`, `WLTrialPersonalDetailsPage`, `WLTrialConsentPage`, `WLTrialNotAvailable`, `WLTrialPackSelection` - **Shared components**: `EligibleProductsList` with click-to-view navigation back to `ProductRecommendation` - **Sticky footer**: Animates in via Framer Motion spring when the at-home treatment banner scrolls out of view - **Lab test tooltip**: Portal-based tooltip on the inclusions list showing HbA1c, FBS, TSH, BUA, and CBC details - **Branding**: Added red and yellow color tokens to `globals.css` for ED 2026 theme - **FormRenderer**: Updated to handle WLTrial custom components and eligibility branching logic ## Files Changed - 21 files changed, +2,052 / -143 lines - 5 new page components, 1 new shared component - 2 new SQL migrations (+ rollbacks) ## Test Plan - [ ] Verify WL trial assessment flow end-to-end: landing → personal details → consent → eligibility → trial pack selection → checkout - [ ] Test eligibility branching: ineligible users see `WLTrialNotAvailable`, eligible users see `WLTrialPackSelection` - [ ] Test trial pack sticky footer appears on scroll and hides when scrolling back up - [ ] Test lab test tooltip renders correctly on mobile (right-aligned, not clipped) and desktop - [ ] Test "View full product" navigation from trial pack to `ProductRecommendation` and back - [ ] Test `FormLayoutWithImage` mobile image sizing across different screen sizes - [ ] Run database migrations and verify form template is created correctly

GoRocky/goforms.title: Feature/match ed assessment design
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# PR: Match ED Assessment Design Updates

## Summary

This PR brings the ED assessment forms in line with the updated design, including a new interactive nitrates safety check, UI/UX fixes across shared components, and form flow reordering.

## Changes

### New: Nitrates Check Section
- Added `NitratesCheckSection` custom component with interactive yes/no question, educational accordion, and medication selection modal
- Uses the shared FormRenderer header and progress bar for a continuous form experience
- Supports "Other" nitrate medication with manual review flow
- Migration `048` sets the custom component on the `nitrates-check` DB section

### Form Flow Reordering
- Migration `049` moves Contact Details back to the end of the form (just before client-injected plan/subscription sections), reversing migration 044

### UI/UX Fixes
- **Dialog component**: Fixed black backdrop issue (Tailwind v4 `bg-opacity-*` → `bg-black/50`), added close (X) button via React context
- **Consent page**: Fixed marketing checkbox not toggling (missing `onChange` handler), added fade-in animation to marketing consent section, updated consent page image
- **Contact details header**: Centered GoRocky logo using spacer element
- **Date picker toggle**: Fixed inverted Manual toggle — now ON by default when showing manual input, OFF for spinner
- **Form content width**: Increased `max-w-lg` → `max-w-xl` for regular form sections to prevent text wrapping

### New UI Components
- Added `accordion.tsx` component (used by NitratesCheckSection)

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `NitratesCheckSection.tsx` | New interactive nitrates safety check component |
| `accordion.tsx` | New accordion UI component |
| `dialog.tsx` | Backdrop fix, close button, React context |
| `GoRockyConsentPage.tsx` | Marketing checkbox fix, animation, image update |
| `GoRockyContactDetails.tsx` | Centered logo, updated title/button text |
| `FormRenderer.tsx` | Integrated NitratesCheckSection with shared header/progress bar, wider content area |
| `FormField.tsx` | Fixed date picker manual toggle logic |
| `048_nitrates_custom_component.sql` | DB migration for nitrates custom component |
| `049_move_contact_details_to_end.sql` | DB migration to reorder contact details |

## Testing

- [ ] ED assessment form flows through all sections with correct progress bar
- [ ] Nitrates check: "No" continues, "Yes" opens medication modal
- [ ] Medication modal: selecting known nitrates continues to summary; "Other" only triggers manual review screen
- [ ] Marketing consent checkbox toggles on/off correctly
- [ ] Dialog close button works across all modals
- [ ] Date picker manual toggle: ON = text input (default), OFF = spinner
- [ ] Contact details appears at end of form, just before plan selection
GoRocky/goforms.summary: This PR brings the ED assessment forms in line with the updated design, including a new interactive nitrates safety check, UI/UX fixes across shared components, and form flow reordering.

This improves TypeScript type inference for component props.

GoRocky/goforms.title: Feature/ed subscriptions
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# PR: ED Subscriptions — Personalized Plan Flow + Checkout

## Summary

Adds a full ED (erectile dysfunction) subscription and one-time purchase flow to GoRocky, including a personalized plan experience, product recommendation, plan selection with pricing tiers, and a Shopify-integrated checkout page.

### What's included

- **ED Assessment Summary**: A combined assessment result + product recommendation page showing eligibility, how the medication helps, product details, and alternative product cards. CTA scrolls to the product recommendation section.
- **ED Personalized Plan Loading**: Animated loading screen with staggered checkmarks before entering the recommendation flow.
- **ED Plan Selection**: Full plan selection UI with subscription tiers (3/6/12-month) and one-time purchase options, quantity selector, pricing comparison table, FAQ section, and checkout flow via Shopify cart URLs.
- **ED Checkout Page**: New `/gorocky/checkout` route serving as the ED checkout shell.
- **ED Product Data Layer**: Comprehensive product catalog in `ed-product-data.ts` covering 8 products (Sildenafil 50mg, 100mg, Tadalafil 20mg, Cialis 20mg, Viagra 25/50/100mg, ED Power Bundle) with full pricing for all subscription durations, one-time quantities, Shopify checkout URLs, medication FAQs, and pricing page content.
- **ED Subscription Config**: Configuration for subscription-specific routing and form slug mapping.
- **Database Migrations**: Two migrations to add the ED subscription summary section and clone the GoRocky ED assessment form for subscriptions.
- **Dev Tooling**: Updated autofill fixtures with ED-specific permutations for faster testing.

### Products supported

| Product | One-time | Subscriptions |
|---|---|---|
| Sildenafil 50mg | 6/8/12/24/36 pills | 6 or 12 pills × 1/3/6/12 months |
| Sildenafil 100mg | 6/8/12/24/36 pills | 6 or 12 pills × 1/3/6/12 months |
| Tadalafil 20mg | 4/8/12 pills | 4 or 8 pills × 1/3/6/12 months |
| Cialis 20mg | 4/8/12 pills | No subscriptions |
| Viagra 25mg | 4/8/12 pills | No subscriptions |
| Viagra 50mg | 4/8/12 pills | No subscriptions |
| Viagra 100mg | 4/8/12 pills | No subscriptions |
| ED Power Bundle | 12/24 pills | No subscriptions |

### Key files

| File / Directory | What it does |
|---|---|
| `src/components/forms/gorocky/personalized-plan/` | All ED personalized plan components (summary, loading, plan selection) |
| `src/components/forms/gorocky/personalized-plan/shared/ed-product-data.ts` | Product catalog, pricing, checkout URLs, FAQs, pricing page content |
| `src/components/forms/gorocky/ed-subscription-config.ts` | ED subscription routing config |
| `src/components/forms/gorocky/GoRockyEDSummaryPage.tsx` | ED summary page component |
| `src/app/gorocky/checkout/page.tsx` | ED checkout route |
| `src/components/forms/FormRenderer.tsx` | Integration of ED personalized plan components into form flow |
| `src/hooks/use-form-template.ts` | Updates to support ED subscription form templates |
| `src/lib/forms/theme-config.ts` | Theme config additions for ED forms |
| `src/lib/test-fixtures/form-fixtures.ts` | Dev autofill fixtures for ED assessment |
| `supabase/migrations/production-forms/041_*` | Add subscription summary section to ED form |
| `supabase/migrations/production-forms/042_*` | Clone ED assessment for subscription variant |

### UI features

- Quantity selector (base vs high quantity) with dynamic pricing updates
- Subscription tier cards with monthly price, total cost, and savings display
- Pricing comparison table showing one-time vs subscription costs per tier
- "Still Unsure? Consult with Our Doctor" modal integration
- Product sleeve images for all 8 products
- Treatment-specific FAQs per product
- Trust badges and impact sections
- Smooth scroll CTA from assessment summary to product recommendation

### Pricing updates

- Corrected Sildenafil 50mg 12-pill subscription pricing (3mo: ₱1,695, 6mo: ₱1,295, 12mo: ₱895)
- Corrected Tadalafil 20mg 4-pill subscription pricing (3mo: ₱1,595, 6mo: ₱1,395)
- All one-time pricing verified against final SRP list
- Removed all "mock pricing" placeholder comments — all values are final

## Test plan

- [ ] Navigate to ED subscription assessment form and complete the assessment flow
- [ ] Verify ED Personalized Plan Loading animation plays correctly
- [ ] Verify ED Assessment Summary shows correct eligibility and product recommendation
- [ ] "See Recommendation" CTA scrolls to product card section
- [ ] Switch between alternative products and verify pricing updates
- [ ] Select base quantity → verify pricing for all subscription tiers
- [ ] Select high quantity → verify pricing for all subscription tiers
- [ ] Select one-time purchase → verify correct pill counts and pricing
- [ ] Pricing comparison table shows correct values with savings (no "save" prefix)
- [ ] Click "Checkout" on subscription tier → verify correct Shopify redirect
- [ ] Click "Checkout" on one-time purchase → verify correct Shopify redirect
- [ ] "Still Unsure? Consult with Our Doctor" opens modal with ₱750 consult link
- [ ] Verify all 8 products display correct sleeve images
- [ ] Verify treatment-specific FAQs render for each product
- [ ] Dev autofill toolbar works with ED-specific fixture data
- [ ] Existing WL and other form flows are unaffected
- [ ] Run database migrations (041, 042) and verify form template is created correctly
- [ ] Verify rollback migrations work cleanly
GoRocky/goforms.summary: Adds a full ED (erectile dysfunction) subscription and one-time purchase flow to GoRocky, including a personalized plan experience, product recommendation, plan selection with pricing tiers, and a Shopify-integrated checkout page.

GoRocky/goforms.title: Fix/hl dob spinner picker
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# Add Toggle Between Spinner and Manual Date Input for GoFuller DOB Field

## Summary
Implemented a toggle switch for the date of birth field in the GoFuller hair loss assessment form, allowing users to choose between a spinner-based date picker and manual text input. The manual input is the default option.

## Changes Made

### 1. FormField Component
**File:** [src/components/forms/FormField.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/goforms/src/components/forms/FormField.tsx:0:0-0:0)

- Added toggle functionality using React `useState` hook for the `date_of_birth` field in `gofuller-assessment` form
- Restructured layout to display:
  - Question label at the top
  - Help text and toggle switch on the same line
  - Conditional rendering of either [SpinnerDatePicker](cci:1://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/goforms/src/components/ui/SpinnerDatePicker.tsx:197:0-353:1) or `TextField` based on toggle state
- Default state: Manual text input (toggle off)
- Both input methods respect DOB validation rules (18-100 years old)

### 2. Switch Component
**File:** [src/components/ui/switch.tsx](cci:7://file:///Users/zoepineda/Desktop/GoRocky/goforms/src/components/ui/switch.tsx:0:0-0:0)

- Created new reusable `Switch` component using Radix UI primitives
- Applied neutral slate-600 color (`#475569`) for:
  - Switch border
  - Checked state background
  - Thumb border (unchecked state)
- Maintains white background for unchecked state and white thumb

## Key Features

- **User Choice**: Toggle between modern spinner UI and traditional manual input
- **Consistent Validation**: Both input methods enforce age requirements (18+ and ≤100 years)
- **Clean UI**: Neutral color scheme with improved layout hierarchy
- **Form-Specific**: Only applies to GoFuller assessment DOB field, other forms unchanged

## Testing Checklist

- [x] Toggle appears only for GoFuller DOB field
- [x] Default state is manual text input
- [x] Switching between modes preserves form state
- [x] Both input methods validate age boundaries correctly
- [x] Form submission works with both input types
- [x] Other date fields remain unaffected

## Dependencies

- `@radix-ui/react-switch` (already in project)
- No new external dependencies added
GoRocky/goforms.summary: Implemented a toggle switch for the date of birth field in the GoFuller hair loss assessment form, allowing users to choose between a spinner-based date picker and manual text input. The manual input is the default option.

GoRocky/goforms.title: Refactor/wl subs update semag pricing
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# PR: Temporary WL Bypass + Semaglutide Dosage Variant Selection + Tirzepatide Pricing Update

## Summary

- **Temporary semaglutide bypass flow**: Users visiting the 3 WL v2 form slugs with `?wl_bypass=semaglutide` skip the full assessment and land directly on the ProductRecommendation screen with pre-seeded form state. The back button is hidden in bypass mode so users cannot navigate to unfilled assessment sections. A sparse submission is created on entry (backend tolerates null name/email/phone).
- **Semaglutide 8mg/16mg dosage variant selection**: ProductRecommendation now renders a variant picker when semaglutide is the active product, allowing users to choose between 8mg ("For first-timers") and 16mg ("For experienced users"). The Medication/Dosage grid is hidden for semaglutide since the variant picker replaces it.
- **Semaglutide sticky footer with total due**: When semaglutide is active, the sticky footer shows "Total Due" with the selected variant price, a "Checkout" CTA, and a "Still Unsure? Consult with Our Doctor" link that opens the ConsultDoctorModal.
- **Semaglutide checkout URL fallback fix**: When per-slug checkout URLs are empty strings, the flow now falls through to PlanSelection instead of silently stopping. Also fixed `getSemaglutideCheckoutUrl` to use `||` instead of `??` so empty strings correctly fall back to the default URL.
- **Refactored semaglutide variant data to data layer**: Moved `SEMAGLUTIDE_VARIANTS`, types, and helper functions from ProductRecommendation component into `recommendation-products.ts` for single-source-of-truth product data.
- **Added compounded-semaglutide to eligible products**: Users viewing tirzepatide now see semaglutide in the "You're also eligible for" section, and vice versa.
- **Display all eligible products in recommendation section**: ProductRecommendation now shows all configured eligible products rather than just one fallback.
- **Reordered PlanSelection tiers**: One-time order option moved above subscription tiers.
- **Form recommender audit doc**: Added `docs/form-recommender-audit.md` documenting the current hard-override state of the recommender.

## Bypass query param

```
?wl_bypass=semaglutide
```

Supported slugs:
- `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2`
- `goslim-assessment-v2`
- `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2-tiktok`

## Files changed

| File | What changed |
|---|---|
| `src/components/forms/FormRenderer.tsx` | WL bypass effect, `isWlBypass` state to hide back button, eligible products seeding |
| `src/components/forms/goslim/personalized-plan/ProductRecommendation.tsx` | Variant picker UI, dynamic subtitle (first-timers/experienced), semaglutide sticky footer with total due + consult, checkout URL fallback to PlanSelection |
| `src/components/forms/goslim/personalized-plan/PlanSelection.tsx` | One-time order moved above subscription tiers |
| `src/components/forms/goslim/personalized-plan/shared/types.ts` | Added type for eligible products |
| `src/lib/personalized-plan/recommendation-products.ts` | Semaglutide variant data, types, and helpers moved here; `getSemaglutideCheckoutUrl` `??` → `||` fix |
| `src/lib/personalized-plan/product-recommender.ts` | Added `compounded-semaglutide` to `eligibleProducts`, hard-override preserved |
| `docs/form-recommender-audit.md` | New audit doc |

## Test plan

- [ ] Visit `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2?wl_bypass=semaglutide` — should skip assessment and land on ProductRecommendation with semaglutide pre-selected
- [ ] Verify back button is hidden on ProductRecommendation in bypass mode
- [ ] Verify back button still works for non-bypass users
- [ ] Select 8mg variant — sticky footer shows ₱10,797, subtitle says "For first-timers"
- [ ] Select 16mg variant — sticky footer shows ₱11,697, subtitle says "For experienced users"
- [ ] Click "Still Unsure? Consult with Our Doctor" — ConsultDoctorModal opens
- [ ] Click "Checkout" on semaglutide — if per-slug URL is blank, should proceed to PlanSelection
- [ ] Switch to tirzepatide via "You're also eligible for" — verify semaglutide appears as eligible option
- [ ] Switch to ozempic/mounjaro — verify Medication/Dosage grid shows, original StickyFooterCTA renders
- [ ] Visit form without `?wl_bypass` — normal assessment flow unchanged
- [ ] Visit non-WL form with `?wl_bypass=semaglutide` — bypass ignored, normal flow
- [ ] Repeat above for `goslim-assessment-v2` and `gorocky-wl-assessment-v2-tiktok`

## Known gaps

- **Per-slug semaglutide checkout URLs are still blank** — bypass and non-bypass semaglutide users on the 3 v2 slugs will flow into PlanSelection rather than direct checkout until these are populated
- **Bypass submissions are sparse** — no name, email, phone, BMI, or medical history; ops must verify externally
- **This is intentionally temporary** — the bypass code is localized and clearly marked for easy removal

The back button is hidden in bypass mode so users cannot navigate to unfilled assessme

GoRocky/goforms.title: Fix/goforms hl subscriptions v1 feedback
GoRocky/goforms.description:
## Summary

Batch of UI/UX improvements and business logic updates for the GoFuller hair loss personalized plan flow, based on user feedback.

### Recommendation Logic
- Updated product recommendation mapping: B (simple), C (affordable), E (recommend) → Finasteride; A (aggressive) → Finoxidil Capsule; D (minimal) → Minoxidil Capsule
- Finoxidil Spray removed as standalone product — now only available as a discounted add-on for 6 and 12-month tiers
- Added Finoxidil Spray add-on eligibility to Finasteride and Minoxidil Capsule products (previously only on Finoxidil Capsule)

### Form Simplification
- Removed "Unsure" option from HIV and genetics questions
- Removed "Other" option from hair loss goals (and its conditional follow-up section)
- DB migration + rollback included

### Plan Selection UX
- Discount code input only visible for one-time orders
- Added slide-in animation for discount code input
- Hidden "₱500 OFF Specialist Consult" inclusion for 3-month tier
- Improved add-on upsell card layout (stacked on mobile, side-by-side on larger screens)

### Product Recommendation Page
- Single-ingredient cards now display full-width with centered content (both ingredients and science cards sections)
- Added open/close animation for doctor consult modal (backdrop fade + spring slide-up)
- Click-outside-to-close and X button for citation popup

### Navigation & Polish
- Fixed scroll-to-top when navigating between personalized plan sections
- Province selector now displays Metro Manila first
- Various responsive layout improvements for testimonials carousel

## Test Plan
- [ ] Complete GoFuller assessment as male with each treatment preference (A-E) and verify correct product recommendation
- [ ] Complete as female and verify Minoxidil Capsule is always recommended
- [ ] Verify Finoxidil Spray does NOT appear in "You're also eligible for" section
- [ ] Verify Finoxidil Spray add-on card appears on 6 and 12-month tiers for all 3 products
- [ ] Verify discount code input only shows for one-time orders
- [ ] Verify "₱500 OFF Specialist Consult" hidden on 3-month tier, visible on 6 and 12-month
- [ ] Verify single-ingredient product cards are full-width and centered
- [ ] Test doctor consult modal animation (open/close)
- [ ] Test citation popup click-outside dismiss and X button
- [ ] Verify scroll resets to top when navigating between plan sections
- [ ] Check province dropdown shows Metro Manila first
GoRocky/goforms.summary: Batch of UI/UX improvements and business logic updates for the GoFuller hair loss personalized plan flow, based on user feedback.

GoRocky/goforms.title: Feature/subscriptions personalized plan
GoRocky/goforms.description:
# Summary
- Adds a complete personalized treatment plan flow after the GoSlim assessment form, guiding users through product recommendation, plan selection, and checkout
- Implements a product recommendation engine that matches users to one of 4 GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Mounjaro, Compounded Semaglutide, Compounded Tirzepatide) based on BMI, weight loss goals, and budget preferences
- Introduces the GoRocky 2026 brand design system with new color palette, Radio Canada font, and refreshed UI components

# What's included
## Product Recommendation Engine (src/lib/personalized-plan/)
- product-recommender.ts — Decision matrix using BMI threshold (35), body loss percentage (15%), and budget preference to select drug family (semaglutide vs tirzepatide) and variant (branded vs compounded)
recommendation-products.ts — Product catalog with pricing tiers, comparison tables, FAQs, dosage info, and installment plan configurations for all 4 medications
- weight-projection.ts — BMI-based weight loss projection calculator for the results visualization
## Personalized Plan Flow (4 screens)
- PersonalizedPlanLoading.tsx — Animated loading screen with progress indicators while "analyzing" the assessment
- YourAssessment.tsx — Summary of the user's assessment answers before showing results
- ProductRecommendation.tsx — Product detail page with hero images, weight loss chart, medication FAQs, testimonials, plan inclusions, and bidirectional navigation to the eligible alternative product
- PlanSelection.tsx — Subscription tier selection (3/6/12 month plans + one-time order) with pricing comparison table, package inclusions, SCOPE specialist consult badges, and checkout integration
## Shared Components (personalized-plan/shared/)
- WeightLossChart — Animated SVG chart showing projected weight loss over 12 months
- FAQAccordion — Reusable accordion with Framer Motion animations
- TestimonialsSection — Before/after testimonial carousel
- CustomerSupportSection — Care team display with avatars
- PlanInclusionsSection — Package feature breakdown
- StickyFooterCTA — Persistent bottom CTA bar
- TrustBadgesSection — LegitScript, NPC Seal, TrustMark badges
- DoctorAttribution — Dr. McKelvie attribution block
## Form Integration
- Extended FormRenderer.tsx with personalized plan section routing, treatment preference fields, and recommendation calculation trigger
- Added 2 database migrations for new form sections (personalized plan + treatment preferences)
## Design System Updates
- GoRocky 2026 color palette (--color-ed-2026-*) in globals.css
- Radio Canada font family with Regular, Medium, SemiBold, Bold weights
- Custom Phosphor-style inline SVG icons replacing Lucide icons in key sections

GoRocky/core.title: fix: back button for custom components not working in ed form
GoRocky/core.description:
# fix(forms): unstick Back button after custom-component sections on ED form

## Problem

On `gorocky-ed-assessment-v2`, the **Back button was dead on the section right after `consent`** (and on any section whose previous visible section was `consent`). Users on `ed_duration` couldn't return to the consent screen.

## Root cause — data, not code

`FormNavigationProvider.goToPreviousSection` ([apps/web/src/components/forms/providers/FormNavigationProvider.tsx:188-191](apps/web/src/components/forms/providers/FormNavigationProvider.tsx#L188-L191)) walks backwards past any section flagged `metadata.isContentOnly` so users never land on a pure transition screen with no Back button:

```ts
let targetIndex = currentSectionIndex - 1;
while (targetIndex >= 0 && visibleSections[targetIndex]?.metadata?.isContentOnly) {
  targetIndex--;
}
```

[Migration 276](apps/backend/src/database/migrations/276_update_ed_assessment_goforms_design.sql) seeded `landing`, `consent`, and `contact_details` with `"isContentOnly": true`. But:

- `GoRockyConsentPage` and `GoRockyContactDetails` are **interactive screens** that collect form data and render their own `FormNavbar` + Back button.
- `landing` sits at index 0 — Back can legitimately return to it.

So the skip-loop ate all three. The comment above the loop even states the *intent* — *"Interactive custom screens (landing, consent, contact) are navigable normally"* — confirming the data is what drifted.

Trace on Back from `ed_duration`:
- `targetIndex=1` → `consent` (isContentOnly) → skip
- `targetIndex=0` → `landing` (isContentOnly) → skip
- `targetIndex=-1` → `visibleSections[-1]` undefined → **no navigation**

## Fix

[`apps/backend/src/database/migrations/359_ed_form_back_button_content_only_fix.sql`](apps/backend/src/database/migrations/359_ed_form_back_button_content_only_fix.sql) strips `isContentOnly` from `landing` / `consent` / `contact_details` on both `gorocky-ed-assessment` and `gorocky-ed-assessment-v2`. Genuinely content-only screens (`confidence_transition`, `nitrates_disqualification`) keep the flag.

Rollback at [`rollback/359_ed_form_back_button_content_only_fix_down.sql`](apps/backend/src/database/migrations/rollback/359_ed_form_back_button_content_only_fix_down.sql).

## Side-effect audit

`isContentOnly` has only two consumers in the repo:

1. **Back-nav skip-loop** — fixed (the intended behavioral change).
2. **Prefill-banner suppression** in [DSFormView.tsx:128](apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/DSFormView.tsx#L128), [LegacyFormView.tsx:60](apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/LegacyFormView.tsx#L60), [FormRendererCore.tsx:816](apps/web/src/components/forms/renderer/FormRendererCore.tsx#L816) — no-op. The banner only renders in the standard-section branch; custom-component sections render via `CustomComponentRenderer` and never show the banner regardless of the flag.

**Backend validation** ([validation.service.ts:91-95](apps/backend/src/modules/forms/services/validation.service.ts#L91-L95)) iterates `section.questions` directly and ignores `isContentOnly` — these sections still have zero rows in `form_questions`, so they remain naturally skipped. Contact-info presence is still enforced by the dedicated check in [forms.service.ts](apps/backend/src/modules/forms/services/forms.service.ts) keyed on `template.customComponents.has('GoRockyContactDetails')`, not on the flag. Also refreshed the comment there so it explains the validation skip via "zero form_questions rows" rather than the flag.

## Apply

```bash
pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate
```

## Files changed

- `apps/backend/src/database/migrations/359_ed_form_back_button_content_only_fix.sql` (new)
- `apps/backend/src/database/migrations/rollback/359_ed_form_back_button_content_only_fix_down.sql` (new)
- `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/services/forms.service.ts` (comment refresh)

Users on `ed_duration` couldn't return to the consent screen. `FormNavigationProvider.goToPreviousSection` ([apps/web/src/components/for

GoRocky/core.title: Fix/blog cms feedback
GoRocky/core.description: # Blog CMS — marketing feedback + Introduction CTA **Branch:** `fix/blog-cms-feedback` Addresses the latest round of marketing feedback on the Blog CMS, adds an Introduction CTA feature, and ships an autosave for the editor. Spans the backend (`apps/backend`), the admin CMS (`apps/admin`), and the public renderer (`apps/webflow`). ## Feedback addressed **1. Flip the position of Article References and About GoRocky.** The public post page now renders **About GoRocky first, then Article References**. The references block was moved out of the main content body and into its own section after the About GoRocky card, keeping the same collapsible styling and column alignment. Before: After: **2. Tab title should reflect the actual page title, not the website title.** The browser tab `` now derives from the post's own title instead of falling back to the site-wide default. Fixed on both the published route (`/blog/[slug]`) and the draft preview route (`/blog/preview/[token]`) — the preview page previously had no per-post title at all, so it showed the site default. <img width="944" height="521" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f4c4009b-7d19-4f60-ac01-b983bc906687" /> **3. Remove the main image from the blog post body.** The main image that sat between Key Takeaways and the Introduction has been removed from the rendered post, so the reader flows straight from Key Takeaways into the introduction. The `mainImage` field is **kept** in the CMS and data model — it's still used as the OG/social-share image fallback — it just no longer renders inline in the article. Before: <img width="1194" height="723" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ae4440c-1f35-4d05-8aae-3f9f26e59435" /> After: <img width="1190" height="723" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7d6fb13-8a93-45a0-b3f0-cc390b48518c" /> **4. Autosave changes as you work.** The editor now autosaves existing posts: it watches all form fields and, 3 seconds after the last edit, saves in the background (with scroll/focus preserved across the save). No more losing work on an accidental back navigation. New (unsaved) posts still require an explicit first save. **5. Add a CTA section after the introduction.** New **Introduction CTA** — an optional CTA card rendered at the end of the post introduction, before the first section. It reuses the same card component and shape as the existing per-section CTAs (heading, button label, link, optional "Learn More" override). Authored in the admin CMS under the Introduction field; rendered on the public post page. <img width="926" height="445" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c8c8883d-69ac-4446-91e1-fb8e638df6cc" /> ## Additional fixes found during testing - **Autosave validation:** autosave was sending raw form values, so empty number inputs (`mainImageWidth`, `featuredOrder`, etc.) went out as `""` and were rejected by the backend's `@IsInt`/`@Min` validators. The autosave handler now runs values through the same Zod schema the manual save uses, coercing `""` to `undefined` before the request. - **Introduction CTA removal not persisting:** "Remove CTA" set the field to `undefined`, which `JSON.stringify` drops from the request body — so the backend never cleared the column. Removal now sets the field to `null` (schema widened to `.nullish()`), which the backend writes as `NULL`. ## New feature: Introduction CTA — implementation | Layer | Change | | --- | --- | | **DB** | Migration `356_blog_introduction_cta.sql` — adds nullable `introduction_cta JSONB` column to `blog_posts` (+ rollback). | | **Backend** | `introductionCta` wired through `CreateBlogPostDto` (validated, optional), the repository's `SELECT`/`INSERT`/`UPDATE`, and `BlogPostRow`. Same `BlogSectionCtaDto` shape as per-section CTAs. | | **Admin CMS** | `introductionCta` added to the Zod schema and `BlogPost` type; new `IntroductionCtaField` component in the Content tab with Add/Remove CTA controls. | | **Renderer** | `introductionCta` added to the webflow `BlogPost` type and API mapping; rendered via the existing `CtaBar` component after the introduction. | ## Deployment / config notes - **Migration must be run:** `pnpm --filter @gorocky/backend migrate` (`356_blog_introduction_cta.sql`). The backend's `SELECT_COLUMNS` references the new column, so reads/writes will error until it's applied. - **Live-page revalidation:** saving a published post invalidates the public ISR cache via the `/api/revalidate` webhook. This requires `REVALIDATE_SECRET` (matching value in `apps/backend/.env` and the webflow env) and `WEBFLOW_REVALIDATE_URL` to be set. Note: `apps/backend/.env.example` currently documents `WEBFLOW_REVALIDATE_URL=http://localhost:3001`, which is the wrong port — the webflow app runs on **3004**. <!-- codesmith:footer --> --- <a href="https://app.blacksmith.sh/GoRocky/codesmith/core/pr/759"><picture><source media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" srcset="https://pr-comments-assets.blacksmith.sh/codesmith/view-in-codesmith-dark.svg"><source media="(prefers-color-scheme: light)" srcset="https://pr-comments-assets.blacksmith.sh/codesmith/view-in-codesmith-light.svg"><img alt="View in Codesmith" src="https://pr-comments-assets.blacksmith.sh/codesmith/view-in-codesmith-dark.svg"></picture></a> <sup>Need help on this PR? GoRocky/core.title: Feature/admin goform filters
GoRocky/core.description: # Admin Form Submissions — Filter, Sort & UX Overhaul Branch: `feature/admin-goform-filters` → `main` ## Summary This PR overhauls the admin **Form Submissions** page (filters, table header controls, template ranking) and fixes a silent 400 bug that was hiding hundreds of real submissions from the dropdown filter. It also adds a small CRM quality-of-life improvement (customer name → user detail link). ## Changes by area ### 1. Form Submissions filters — compact toolbar `apps/admin/src/app/(authenticated)/forms/submissions/page.tsx` - Replaced the multi-row Card-wrapped filter block with a single-row flex toolbar. - Removed the "Filters" title and stacked labels; labels are now `sr-only`, placeholders carry the affordance. - Date range is now one bordered control: `[📅 From → To]` with an inline arrow and a ✕ icon that only appears when a date is set. - Added a **Clear filters** ghost button (appears only when at least one filter is active) that resets search, category, template, status, and dates in one click. ### 2. Form / Source toggle in the table header `apps/admin/src/app/(authenticated)/forms/submissions/page.tsx` - Replaced the pill `Switch` with a proper segmented control: `Form [ Name | URL ]`. - Uses the standard iOS/shadcn pattern — shared muted track, active option on a raised `bg-background` chip with subtle shadow. - Keyboard- and screen-reader-friendly (`role="group"`, `aria-pressed` on each option). ### 3. Select component — truncation fix `apps/admin/src/components/ui/select.tsx` - `[&>span]:line-clamp-1` was letting long selected values push the chevron around and look off-center. - Swapped to `[&>span]:flex-1 [&>span]:truncate [&>span]:text-left [&>span]:min-w-0` so the value takes available width, truncates with ellipsis, and keeps the chevron pinned to the right regardless of text length. ### 4. Template filter — hidden 400 bug (data-visible fix) `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/dto/submission-admin.dto.ts` **Problem:** Selecting *GoRocky ED Assessment (V2)* (and several other ED/HL templates) returned "0 submissions" even though the DB had 119 rows for it. Root cause: those templates were seeded with hand-typed, non-RFC-4122 UUIDs (invalid version + variant digits). NestJS' `ValidationPipe` + `class-validator`'s `@IsUUID()` rejected the request with 400 before the SQL ever ran. TanStack Query's `data` was `undefined`, which the UI renders as an empty table. **Fix:** Loosened the filter-side `templateId` check from `@IsUUID()` to a shape-only `@Matches(/^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i)`. `userId` stays on strict `@IsUUID()` because BetterAuth generates compliant UUIDs. Values still flow through parameterized SQL — no injection risk. ### 5. Template dropdown sort order `apps/admin/src/app/(authenticated)/forms/submissions/page.tsx` `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/repositories/template.repository.ts` `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/entities/template.entity.ts` `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/dto/template.dto.ts` `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/forms-admin.controller.ts` `apps/admin/src/lib/api/forms.ts` Templates in the dropdown are now ranked by: 1. **Category priority** — Erectile Dysfunction → Weight Management → Hair Loss → everything else (alphabetical) → uncategorized (last). 2. **Submission volume** (desc) within the same category. 3. **Name** (alpha) as the final tiebreaker. Backend plumbing: `getAllTemplates` returns `submissionCount` via a correlated `COUNT(*)` subquery on `form_submissions`; surfaced through `FormTemplate` entity, `TemplateResponseDto`, and the admin API's `TemplateListResponse`. ### 6. Category filter (from earlier in the branch) - New hierarchical filter: picking a **Category** narrows the **Template** dropdown to that category's forms only. - Category options are derived from `form_templates.category_label` (populated by migration 285). ### 7. CRM — clickable customer names `apps/admin/src/app/(authenticated)/crm/customers/page.tsx` - Customer name in the CRM customers table is now a `Link` to `/users/`, matching the pattern already used on the submissions page. ## Files touched - **Admin (frontend)** - `apps/admin/src/app/(authenticated)/forms/submissions/page.tsx` - `apps/admin/src/app/(authenticated)/crm/customers/page.tsx` - `apps/admin/src/components/ui/select.tsx` - `apps/admin/src/lib/api/forms.ts` - **Backend** - `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/dto/submission-admin.dto.ts` - `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/dto/template.dto.ts` - `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/entities/template.entity.ts` - `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/repositories/template.repository.ts` - `apps/backend/src/modules/forms/forms-admin.controller.ts` - **Database** (earlier in the branch) - Migration adding `form_templates.category_label`. ## Deployment notes - Backend restart required for the DTO change (template filter fix) and for `submissionCount` to appear on `/forms/admin/templates`. - No data migration needed for the UUID fix — it's purely a validator relaxation. - The existing seed/legacy templates with non-compliant UUIDs can be left as-is. Long-term, consider regenerating them with real UUIDs and running `UPDATE form_submissions SET template_id = WHERE template_id = ` to avoid further surprises. ## Testing checklist - [ ] Admin → Forms → Submissions: all filters live on one row on desktop; wrap cleanly on narrow viewports. - [ ] Changing any filter → **Clear filters** button appears and resets state on click. - [ ] Date range: setting either date enables the ✕ clear; clearing hides it again. - [ ] Picking **GoRocky ED Assessment (V2)** returns all 119 submissions (previously 0). - [ ] Picking **GoRocky ED Assessment (Full)**, **GoRocky ED Assessment**, and **ED Assessment** returns their rows too. - [ ] Template dropdown order: ED templates first (busiest at top), then WL, then HL, then rest. - [ ] `[ Name | URL ]` toggle flips the Form column between template name and source URL; active side is highlighted. - [ ] Long select values (e.g. "GoRocky ED Assessment (V2)") truncate with ellipsis and keep the chevron flush right. - [ ] CRM → Customers: clicking a customer's name navigates to `/users/`. ## Screenshots to attach Please include the following in the PR description (before/after where noted): 1. **Filters toolbar — before vs after** - *Before:* Card-wrapped two-row filter block with labels above each input. - *After:* Single-row compact toolbar with inline date range and clear-filters button. 2. **Clear filters button** 3. **Date range control** — showing the `From → To` inline layout and the ✕ clear icon when a date is set. 4. **Form / Source segmented toggle** — two screenshots: `Name` active and `URL` active in the Form column header, plus a row showing the URL rendering. 5. **Template dropdown order** — dropdown open, showing ED templates at the top, sorted by submission count; WL/HL/others below. 6. **Template filter works again** — "GoRocky ED Assessment (V2)" selected, table populated with submissions and the count banner showing `119 submissions found for "GoRocky ED Assessment (V2)"`. 7. **Select truncation fix — before vs after** — a long template label in the filter trigger, demonstrating proper left-aligned ellipsis and right-pinned chevron. 8. **Category → Template hierarchy** 9. **CRM customer link** — hovering a customer row, name underlined; click-through to `/users/` in the next frame. ## Out of scope / follow-ups - Regenerate the four legacy seed templates with RFC-4122-compliant UUIDs and migrate their submissions. This avoids relying on the relaxed validator for any new endpoints we add. - Add a "busiest templates" summary card on the Submissions page (now cheap since `submissionCount` is already on the list endpoint).
GoRocky/core.summary: This PR overhauls the admin **Form Submissions** page (filters, table header controls, template ranking) and fixes a silent 400 bug that was hiding hundreds of real submissions from the dropdown filter. It also adds a small CRM quality-of-life improvement (customer name → user detail link).

GoRocky/gorocky-erp.title: fix: hard-filter doctor assignment by risk level
GoRocky/gorocky-erp.description:
## Summary
- **Hard-filter by risk level**: Doctor assignment now excludes doctors whose `risk_levels` don't match the order's risk level. Previously, risk level was only a soft priority boost with a fallback to any category-matched doctor.
- **Auto re-assignment on risk change**: New `POST /api/orders/reassign-doctors-on-risk-change` endpoint re-assigns doctors for pending orders (`raw_order`, `in_preparation`) when a customer's risk level changes.
- **Edge case handling**: If no doctor matches after a force-reassign, `doctor_id` is set to `null` with an audit trail in `doctor_assignment_reason`.
- **Backfill script**: One-off script (`scripts/fix-mismatched-doctor-assignments.js`) to find and fix existing mismatched assignments in production.

### Files changed
- `src/orders/services/validate-and-assign.service.ts` — removed fallback logic, added `reassignDoctorsOnRiskLevelChange()` and `clearOrderItemDoctor()`
- `src/orders/controllers/validate-and-assign.controller.ts` — added reassignment endpoint with `@IsIn` risk level validation
- `src/orders/services/validate-and-assign.service.spec.ts` — 10 unit tests covering hard filter, reassignment, and edge cases
- `scripts/fix-mismatched-doctor-assignments.js` — one-off backfill script with `--dry-run` support

## Test plan
- [x] Run `yarn test` — all 10 new unit tests pass
- [x] Verify on staging: assign a doctor to an order where risk levels don't match → should get no assignment (null)
- [x] Verify on staging: change a customer's risk level → pending orders should be re-assigned
- [x] Run backfill script with `--dry-run` on prod to preview mismatches before applying
GoRocky/gorocky-erp.summary: **Hard-filter by risk level**: Doctor assignment now excludes doctors whose `risk_levels` don't match the order's risk level. Previously, risk level was only a soft priority boost with a fallback to any category-matched doctor. **Auto re-assignment on risk change**: New `POST /api/orders/reassign-do
